title: Mimicry: Towards the Reproducibility of GAN Research
lineage_note: A lightweight PyTorch GAN library (kwotsin/mimicry) providing reproducible baselines and FID/IS evaluation for popular GANs (DCGAN, WGAN, SNGAN, SSGAN, InfoMax-GAN). Modest direct citations but notable as reproducibility infrastructure; first author Kwot Sin Lee went on to be an ML engineer/researcher at Snap Research (co-authoring works like AV-Link) and later xAI.
author_followups: Kwot Sin Lee -> Snap Research generative video/audio work (e.g., AV-Link) and subsequently xAI
InfoMax-GAN (Lee et al., WACV 2021) by the same first author
notable_citers: Various GAN reproducibility / benchmarking and StyleGAN follow-up studies citing the library

title: Object-Centric Image Generation from Layouts
lineage_note: This AI4CC abstract corresponds to OC-GAN, later published as a full paper at AAAI 2021, introducing the Scene-Graph Similarity Module (SGSM) for layout-to-image generation and outperforming LostGAN on COCO-Stuff and Visual Genome. Authored by Yoshua Bengio (Turing Award) and Devon Hjelm; it became a standard baseline in the layout-to-image / scene-graph generation line that fed into later controllable text-to-image and diffusion layout work.
author_followups: Full AAAI 2021 paper 'Object-Centric Image Generation from Layouts' (OC-GAN)
Bengio/Hjelm continued representation-learning and generative modeling lines (e.g., infomax/representation work)
notable_citers: Context-Aware Layout to Image Generation with Enhanced Object Appearance (CVPR 2021)
Subsequent layout-to-image and scene-graph-to-image generation surveys and methods

title: Freeze the Discriminator: A Simple Baseline for Fine-Tuning GANs
lineage_note: FreezeD established a simple, strong, and very widely adopted baseline for transfer learning / few-shot adaptation of GANs (freeze lower discriminator layers), demonstrated on StyleGAN and SNGAN-projection. It became a standard comparison point in the GAN domain-adaptation and few-shot generation literature; authors Sangwoo Mo, Minsu Cho, and Jinwoo Shin run prolific KAIST/POSTECH generative-modeling labs. Citation count not retrievable here (S2 API rate-limited), but it is a heavily cited baseline.
author_followups: Mo, Cho, Shin continued GAN/diffusion and representation-learning research (e.g., MineGAN-related and few-shot adaptation follow-ups, contrastive learning work)
notable_citers: Few-shot Image Generation via Cross-domain Correspondence (Ojha et al., CVPR 2021)
StyleGAN domain-adaptation / few-shot GAN transfer papers using FreezeD as a baseline

title: SegAttnGAN: Text to Image Generation with Segmentation Attention
lineage_note: Won the AI4CC 2020 Audience Choice award; adds segmentation guidance to attentional text-to-image GANs (building on AttnGAN) and reports improved Inception Scores on CUB and Oxford-102. An incremental but solid contribution in the pre-diffusion text-to-image GAN era; from PAII Inc. (Ping An), with first author Yuchuan Gou later moving to Zoox. No strong famous-lineage successor identified.
author_followups: Authors (PAII/Ping An) continued applied vision/ML work; first author Yuchuan Gou later at Zoox (ML platform)
notable_citers: Later text-to-image GAN surveys and segmentation/semantic-guided T2I methods (e.g., Adma-GAN, diversity-regularized T2I)

title: Toward High-quality Few-shot Font Generation with Dual Memory
lineage_note: This AI4CC runner-up corresponds to DM-Font, published at ECCV 2020, a foundational few-shot compositional font generation method (dual memory + compositionality) from Clova AI/NAVER. It directly seeded a prominent line of few-shot font generation work by the same NAVER authors: LF-Font (AAAI 2021) and MX-Font (ICCV 2021), making it a clear precursor within the few-shot font-generation subfield.
author_followups: LF-Font: Few-shot Font Generation with Localized Style Representations and Factorization (Park et al., AAAI 2021)
MX-Font: Multiple Heads Are Better Than One (Park et al., ICCV 2021)
Sanghyuk Chun's broader CLOVA/NAVER work (e.g., dataset/representation papers)
notable_citers: LF-Font and MX-Font (NAVER follow-ups)
Subsequent few-shot/compositional font generation methods (e.g., FsFont, CG-GAN) and surveys

title: Generating Object Stamps
lineage_note: A GAN method to generate foreground object masks+textures and composite them into backgrounds (Brown Visual Computing). It directly preceded GaussiGAN (BMVC/CVPRW 2021) from the same group, and is notable for its author roster: Aaron Gokaslan later became a prominent open-AI figure (OpenWebText, CommonCanvas CVPR 2024, BLOOM/DBRX, Mozilla Rise25 honoree), and Oliver Wang (Adobe -> Google) is a well-known generative-imaging researcher.
author_followups: GaussiGAN (Mejjati et al., BMVC/CVPRW 2021) by the same Brown group
Aaron Gokaslan -> OpenWebText, CommonCanvas (CVPR 2024), CommonPile, contributions to BLOOM/DBRX
Oliver Wang -> numerous Adobe/Google generative video and image-editing works
notable_citers: Image composition / object insertion and copy-paste augmentation papers
Layout-to-image and controllable generation follow-ups citing compositional object generation

title: COCO-FUNIT: Few-Shot Unsupervised Image Translation with a Content Conditioned Style Encoder
lineage_note: Direct successor to NVIDIA's FUNIT, fixing its 'content loss' problem via a content-conditioned style encoder; co-authored by Ming-Yu Liu, who leads NVIDIA's Deep Imagination group. It sits squarely in the NVIDIA generative-image line that produced GauGAN/SPADE, StyleGAN-adjacent translation work, and later large text-to-image diffusion (eDiff-I), making it a precursor node in a landmark generative-AI lineage.
author_followups: Ming-Yu Liu went on to lead NVIDIA work including GauGAN/SPADE, StyleGAN-based editing, and eDiff-I (text-to-image diffusion, 2022)
Kuniaki Saito (with advisor Kate Saenko) continued influential domain-adaptation work (OVANet ICCV 2021, DANCE) and later joined OMRON SINIC X
notable_citers: StyleGAN-based image translation and few-shot translation surveys
NVIDIA follow-on image-to-image translation papers

title: RetrieveGAN: Image Synthesis via Differentiable Patch Retrieval
lineage_note: An early retrieval-augmented image synthesis method (differentiable patch retrieval) that anticipates the later retrieval-augmented generation trend in vision. Authored by a strong UC Merced (Ming-Hsuan Yang lab) and Google team whose members went on to Meta, Snap Research, and Google generative-vision teams.
author_followups: Hung-Yu Tseng became a Research Scientist at Meta (continued generative/3D-4D synthesis work)
Hsin-Ying Lee joined Snap Research Creative Vision (2D/3D/4D generation), later Meta
Lu Jiang and Weilong Yang continued large-scale generative video/image work at Google
notable_citers: Retrieval-augmented image generation and diffusion works (e.g., RDM / retrieval-augmented diffusion lineage)
Ming-Hsuan Yang lab follow-on synthesis papers

title: Semantic Hierarchy Emerges in Deep Generative Representations for Scene Synthesis
lineage_note: By Yujun Shen, Ceyuan Yang, and Bolei Zhou — the GenForce group that produced the landmark GAN latent-space interpretability line. It is a sibling/precursor to InterFaceGAN (CVPR 2020) and SeFa (CVPR 2021), foundational works (each hundreds-to-thousands of citations) on understanding and editing GAN latent spaces that shaped modern controllable generation.
author_followups: Yujun Shen & Bolei Zhou: InterFaceGAN (CVPR 2020) and SeFa/Closed-Form Factorization (CVPR 2021), cornerstone GAN-editing papers
GenForce open-source toolkit for GAN analysis/editing widely adopted
Bolei Zhou's broader interpretability and generative-representation research program
notable_citers: InterFaceGAN
GANSpace
StyleGAN-based semantic editing and latent-space disentanglement works

title: Conditional Image Generation and Manipulation for User-Specified Content
lineage_note: A University of Amsterdam workshop-style paper on user-specified conditional image generation/manipulation; modest citation count and no major published venue beyond arXiv. It reads as an early text/attribute-conditioned editing exploration rather than a precursor to a specific landmark line.
author_followups: David Stap subsequently worked on machine translation / NLP at the University of Amsterdam
Authors did not produce a clearly traceable landmark generative-vision follow-up from this line
notable_citers: Text-conditioned image generation and editing surveys

title: FaR-GAN for One-Shot Face Reenactment
lineage_note: A one-shot face-reenactment GAN from Edward Delp's Purdue media-forensics lab; low citation count and arXiv-only. Its main lineage is within face reenactment / deepfake-and-detection research rather than a landmark generative model.
author_followups: The Purdue Delp group (Hao, Baireddy, Reibman, Delp) continued media forensics / deepfake detection and synthetic-media analysis work
notable_citers: One-shot / few-shot face reenactment and talking-head synthesis surveys

title: CalliGAN: Style and Structure-aware Chinese Calligraphy Character Generator
lineage_note: A well-cited (for an arXiv-only AI4CC paper) method that decomposes Chinese characters into structural components for style-and-structure-aware calligraphy generation. It became a frequently referenced baseline in the niche but active Chinese-font / calligraphy generation literature.
author_followups: NTU group (Jane Yung-jen Hsu lab) continued HCI/ML work; CalliGAN remains the team's notable contribution to font/calligraphy generation
notable_citers: Chinese font generation works (e.g., component-based and diffusion-based font generators such as FontDiffuser / MX-Font-style follow-ups)
Few-shot font/calligraphy style transfer surveys

title: Network Fusion for Content Creation with Conditional INNs
later_venue: CVPR Workshop (AI for Content Creation) / arXiv
lineage_note: A small workshop paper by Patrick Esser, Robin Rombach, and Bjorn Ommer (Heidelberg / Ommer Lab) on conditional invertible neural networks for content creation. This exact trio is the core of the team that went on to create VQGAN/Taming Transformers (CVPR 2021) and, decisively, Latent Diffusion Models / Stable Diffusion (CVPR 2022) — one of the most impactful generative models ever — after which Rombach, Esser (and Blattmann, Lorenz) joined Stability AI and later co-founded Black Forest Labs (FLUX). Despite trivial direct citations, its authorship lineage is landmark-tier.
author_followups: Taming Transformers for High-Resolution Image Synthesis (VQGAN), Esser/Rombach/Ommer, CVPR 2021
High-Resolution Image Synthesis with Latent Diffusion Models (Stable Diffusion), Rombach/Blattmann/Lorenz/Esser/Ommer, CVPR 2022
Founding of Stability AI Stable Diffusion releases and Black Forest Labs (FLUX) by Rombach, Esser, Blattmann, Lorenz
notable_citers: Latent Diffusion Models / Stable Diffusion (same authors, foundational T2I model)

title: Text-guided Image Manipulation via Local Feature Editing
later_venue: ACM Multimedia 2021 (as 'Text as Neural Operator: Image Manipulation by Text Instruction', TIM-GAN)
lineage_note: This is the AI4CC workshop precursor to the Google Research paper 'Text as Neural Operator: Image Manipulation by Text Instruction' (TIM-GAN, arXiv:2008.04556), later published at ACM Multimedia 2021. Authors include Lu Jiang (a prominent Google/ByteDance generative-video researcher, later behind VideoPoet and Lumiere-adjacent efforts), Honglak Lee, and Irfan Essa — an early instruction-based local image-editing GAN method that prefigures the instruction-driven editing paradigm later popularized by diffusion methods (InstructPix2Pix). Semantic Scholar was rate-limiting so the exact citation count could not be confirmed; not fabricated.
author_followups: Text as Neural Operator: Image Manipulation by Text Instruction (TIM-GAN), ACM Multimedia 2021
Lu Jiang's subsequent generative-video work at Google (VideoPoet) and ByteDance
US Patent 11562518B2 'Image manipulation by text instruction' (Google)
notable_citers: Subsequent text-guided / instruction-based image editing surveys and methods

title: Learning to Shadow Hand-drawn Sketches
lineage_note: A focused CVPR 2020 method (UMBC + Project HAT) that generates detailed, controllable artistic shadows for hand-drawn line art given a desired light direction. Modestly cited and useful within the anime/illustration and sketch-rendering niche; no landmark follow-on lineage, though it remains a reference for learning-based art shadowing/relighting of line drawings.
notable_citers: Subsequent sketch/line-art shading and reference-based colorization works

title: SEAN: Image Synthesis with Semantic Region-Adaptive Normalization
lineage_note: A CVPR 2020 Oral from KAUST (Peihao Zhu, Rameen Abdal, Yipeng Qin, Peter Wonka) that introduced semantic region-adaptive normalization, extending SPADE/GauGAN to allow per-region style control from reference images — a widely adopted building block for semantic image synthesis and style transfer (~280 citations, 50 highly influential). The same KAUST/Wonka group produced the influential Image2StyleGAN inversion line (Abdal et al.), making this part of a strong GAN-editing research lineage.
author_followups: Image2StyleGAN / Image2StyleGAN++ (Abdal, Qin, Wonka), GAN inversion
Barbershop and StyleGAN-based editing work from the KAUST/Wonka group
notable_citers: Numerous semantic image synthesis and GAN-based editing papers building on SPADE/SEAN normalization

title: MixNMatch: Multifactor Disentanglement and Encoding for Conditional Image Generation
lineage_note: A CVPR 2020 conditional generative model (Yuheng Li, Krishna Kumar Singh, Utkarsh Ojha, Yong Jae Lee; UC Davis / Wisconsin AI Vision lab) that disentangles and encodes background, pose, shape, and texture from real images with minimal supervision for mix-and-match generation. Its lead authors went on to landmark generative work — the same Yuheng Li and Yong Jae Lee co-authored GLIGEN (Grounded Language-to-Image Generation, CVPR 2023), a widely used controllable diffusion method — placing this paper in an important lab lineage. Semantic Scholar was rate-limiting so the exact citation count could not be confirmed; not fabricated.
author_followups: GLIGEN: Open-Set Grounded Text-to-Image Generation (Yuheng Li, Yong Jae Lee et al.), CVPR 2023
Krishna Kumar Singh's generative work at Adobe Research
Utkarsh Ojha's work on detecting GAN/diffusion-generated images
notable_citers: Disentangled / controllable image generation papers
Fine-grained conditional generation follow-ups citing the mix-and-match factor framework

title: Interpreting the Latent Space of GANs for Semantic Face Editing
lineage_note: InterFaceGAN (Yujun Shen, Jinjin Gu, Xiaoou Tang, Bolei Zhou; CUHK/genforce) is a foundational and very highly cited (~1,080 citations, 186 highly influential) work showing that GAN latent spaces encode disentangled, linearly-separable semantic attributes (age, gender, pose, glasses), enabling controllable face editing on any pretrained StyleGAN/PGGAN. It seeded an entire latent-space-editing subfield: the same authors' SeFa (Closed-Form Factorization, CVPR 2021) and the TPAMI InterFaceGAN extension, alongside contemporaneous GANSpace, with first author Yujun Shen later a leading generative-model researcher at Ant Group and Bolei Zhou now faculty at UCLA. Landmark lineage.
author_followups: InterFaceGAN: Interpreting the Disentangled Face Representation Learned by GANs, TPAMI 2020 (extended journal version)
Closed-Form Factorization of Latent Semantics in GANs (SeFa), Shen & Zhou, CVPR 2021
In-Domain GAN Inversion for Real Image Editing (genforce), ECCV 2020
notable_citers: GANSpace (Harkonen et al., NeurIPS 2020)
StyleFlow, StyleCLIP and many StyleGAN-editing / latent-direction discovery papers
Numerous GAN inversion and image-editing surveys

title: StarGAN v2: Diverse Image Synthesis for Multiple Domains
author_followups: The same Clova AI / NAVER team (Yunjey Choi, Youngjung Uh, Jung-Woo Ha) continued StyleGAN-family research; Choi remained a leading generative-models researcher at NAVER AI Lab with later work such as 'Visual Style Prompting with Swapping Self-Attention' (2024).
StarGAN v2 is the direct successor to the team's own StarGAN (CVPR 2018), forming a landmark lineage in multi-domain image-to-image translation.
notable_citers: StyleGAN2 / StyleGAN-family follow-ups
Numerous image-to-image translation and diffusion-era image-editing papers
lineage_note: A landmark generative-models paper (CVPR 2020, ~2,080 citations) that became a canonical baseline for diverse, multi-domain image translation and introduced the widely reused AFHQ animal-faces dataset. Its open-source release by Clova AI/NAVER made it a heavily-built-upon foundation in the GAN-to-diffusion editing lineage.

title: SieveNet: A Unified Framework for Robust Image-Based Virtual Try-On
author_followups: Ayush Chopra (then Adobe MDSR) went on to MIT Media Lab, where he develops 'Large Population Models' / agent-based simulation frameworks (AAMAS 2024), a notably different and prominent research direction.
Kumar Ayush (Stanford at the time) and the Adobe MDSR group (Krishnamurthy, Hemani) continued producing virtual try-on and generative fashion work.
notable_citers: Subsequent image-based virtual try-on papers (follow-ups to the CP-VTON/VITON line)
Diffusion-based try-on works that benchmark against coarse-to-fine warping baselines
lineage_note: A solid WACV 2020 virtual try-on paper (~72 citations) introducing a multi-stage coarse-to-fine warping network with a perceptual geometric matching loss; a recognized step in the VITON/CP-VTON try-on lineage rather than a landmark. Notable mainly as early work by Ayush Chopra, who moved on to prominent agent-based-simulation research at MIT.

title: Image2StyleGAN++: How to Edit the Embedded Images?
author_followups: Rameen Abdal and Peter Wonka (KAUST) followed this with StyleFlow (ACM TOG 2021), a highly influential attribute-conditioned StyleGAN exploration method, and later StyleGAN-NADA / 3D-aware GAN work — a clear, high-impact lineage in GAN latent-space editing.
Direct successor to the same authors' Image2StyleGAN (ICCV 2019), which established the W+ embedding paradigm.
notable_citers: StyleFlow (Abdal et al., TOG 2021)
pixel2style2pixel (pSp), e4e encoder, and the broad GAN-inversion / StyleGAN-editing literature
lineage_note: Influential StyleGAN-editing paper (CVPR 2020, ~591 citations) that extended the W+ embedding with noise optimization and local edits, helping define the GAN-inversion-for-editing paradigm. Part of a strong lineage: the Image2StyleGAN series directly preceded the authors' widely-cited StyleFlow and the larger GAN-inversion literature.

title: Directional GAN: A Novel Conditioning Strategy for Generative Networks
author_followups: Authors are from Adobe's research/applied teams (Shradha Agrawal, Shankar Venkitachalam, Dhanya Raghu, Deepak Pai); the method fed Adobe's interest in controllable image generation for marketing/graphic-design content, but no specific landmark follow-up by these exact authors was identified.
notable_citers: Surveys on conditional GANs / GAN conditioning strategies (e.g., reviews of conditioning approaches on regression labels)
lineage_note: A lightweight, practical Adobe method that conditions an unconditional generator by moving along attribute directions in latent space (received press coverage via Synced/MarkTechPost). Modest academic impact with no identified landmark successor; an ordinary-tier applied-research contribution. Exact citation count could not be confirmed (Semantic Scholar API was rate-limited and search did not surface a reliable number).

title: Texture Generation with Neural Cellular Automata
later_venue: Distill 'Self-Organising Textures' (distill.pub), part of the Differentiable Self-Organizing Systems thread
author_followups: The same Google team (Alexander Mordvintsev, Eyvind Niklasson, Ettore Randazzo) authored the seminal 'Growing Neural Cellular Automata' (Distill 2020) and went on to muNCA (Ultra-Compact NCA, 2021), Mesh Neural Cellular Automata (ACM TOG 2024), and the broader Distill 'Differentiable Self-Organizing Systems' thread.
This paper corresponds to the influential Distill 'Self-Organising Textures' article and is a core entry in the NCA research program these authors created.
notable_citers: Mesh Neural Cellular Automata (TOG 2024)
Multi-texture synthesis through signal-responsive NCA (Scientific Reports 2025)
The growing body of Neural Cellular Automata research
lineage_note: By the originators of Neural Cellular Automata (the 'Growing NCA' Distill team at Google), this work brought NCA to texture synthesis and is part of the highly influential Distill self-organizing-systems thread that effectively launched the modern NCA subfield. A landmark lineage despite a modest formal arXiv citation count (exact S2 count unconfirmed due to API rate-limiting).

title: Halluci-Net: Scene Completion by Exploiting Object Co-occurrence Relationships
author_followups: Co-author Tejas Gokhale (then ASU PhD student) went on to prominent vision-and-language robustness research, including 'Semantically Distributed Robust Optimization for Vision-and-Language Inference' (SDRO) and 'On the Robustness of Language Guidance for Low-Level Vision Tasks' (CVPR 2024).
Senior authors Pavan Turaga (ASU) and Aswin Sankaranarayanan (CMU) are established imaging/vision researchers; Kuldeep Kulkarni is at Adobe Research India.
notable_citers: Semantic-layout / labelmap-conditioned image-synthesis papers building on pix2pixHD-style pipelines
lineage_note: A two-stage scene-completion method (sparse-to-dense labelmaps feeding pix2pixHD) evaluated on Cityscapes; a competent but niche contribution. Its main lineage value is as early work by Tejas Gokhale, who became a notable figure in vision-language robustness; exact citation count unconfirmed due to Semantic Scholar rate-limiting.

title: Deep Graphics Encoder for Real Time Video Makeup Synthesis From Example
lineage_note: An inverse-graphics approach to makeup transfer from L'Oreal Research / ModiFace and Telecom Paris (Kips, Gori, Perrot, Bloch, plus ModiFace/L'Oreal's Aarabi, Jiang, Phung). It is part of a sustained industrial makeup-AR research line: it follows the same group's CA-GAN (ECCV Workshops 2020) and feeds into the broader makeup-transfer literature that culminates in diffusion-based methods like Stable-Makeup. Notable as applied-research underpinning L'Oreal/ModiFace's shipped virtual try-on technology rather than as an academically high-citation work.
author_followups: CA-GAN: Weakly Supervised Color Aware GAN for Controllable Makeup Transfer (Kips et al., ECCV Workshops 2020) — closely related prior/sibling work by the same core authors
Robin Kips' continued PhD/industry work on makeup synthesis and virtual try-on at L'Oreal/ModiFace
notable_citers: Stable-Makeup: When Real-World Makeup Transfer Meets Diffusion Model (2024)

title: Strumming to the Beat: Audio-Conditioned Contrastive Video Textures
lineage_note: Audio-conditioned contrastive video textures from a star Berkeley/Michigan author group (Narasimhan, Ginosar, Owens, Efros, Darrell). Lead author Medhini Narasimhan went on to highly visible video-understanding work — CLIP-It! (NeurIPS 2021) and TL;DW (ECCV 2022) — during her Berkeley PhD under Trevor Darrell. The co-authors (Efros, Darrell, Owens, Ginosar) are landmark figures, though this specific paper's direct citation impact is moderate.
author_followups: CLIP-It! Language-Guided Video Summarization (Narasimhan et al., NeurIPS 2021)
TL;DW? Summarizing Instructional Videos with Task Relevance and Cross-Modal Saliency (Narasimhan et al., ECCV 2022)
notable_citers: Subsequent audio-visual synthesis and video-texture / sound-conditioned generation literature

title: High-Resolution Complex Scene Synthesis with Transformers
lineage_note: Best-paper at AI4CC 2021 by Jahn, Robin Rombach, and Bjorn Ommer of the CompVis lab — the exact group that produced VQGAN/Taming Transformers and then Latent Diffusion Models / Stable Diffusion (CVPR 2022). This paper builds directly on VQGAN to do layout-conditioned high-res scene synthesis, sitting squarely in the lineage that led to one of the most influential generative models in history. Landmark lineage via its authors and the CompVis generative-modeling trajectory.
author_followups: High-Resolution Image Synthesis with Latent Diffusion Models / Stable Diffusion (Rombach, Blattmann, Lorenz, Esser, Ommer, CVPR 2022)
Taming Transformers for High-Resolution Image Synthesis / VQGAN (Esser, Rombach, Ommer, CVPR 2021) — sibling/foundational work
Robin Rombach co-founding Black Forest Labs (FLUX) lineage of text-to-image models
notable_citers: High-Resolution Image Synthesis with Latent Diffusion Models (Stable Diffusion)
Broad layout-to-image and autoregressive image synthesis literature

title: Learning to Generate Novel Scene Compositions from Single Images and Videos
lineage_note: One-shot / single-image GAN synthesis (a.k.a. One-Shot GAN) from Bosch Research and Bonn (Sushko, Gall, Khoreva). It directly precedes the same authors' OSMIS: One-Shot Synthesis of Images and Segmentation Masks (WACV 2023), extending one-shot generation to joint image-mask synthesis useful for data augmentation. A coherent, notable research line from a strong industrial-academic group, though modest in raw citations.
author_followups: One-Shot Synthesis of Images and Segmentation Masks (OSMIS) (Sushko, Zhang, Gall, Khoreva, WACV 2023)
Continued Bosch Research one-shot/limited-data generative modeling work by Khoreva and Sushko
notable_citers: Single-image and few-shot GAN synthesis literature building on SinGAN-style approaches

title: Automatic Style Transfer for Non-Linear Video Editing
later_venue: arXiv (titled 'Automatic Non-Linear Video Editing Transfer' on Semantic Scholar)
lineage_note: Google Research work (Frey, Peggy Chi, Weilong Yang, Irfan Essa) on transferring editing style for non-linear video editing — part of Google's computational video-editing / video-creation research agenda led by Irfan Essa (Georgia Tech/Google) and HCI researcher Peggy Chi. Sits within a notable applied line of automatic video editing and content-creation tooling rather than a high-citation theoretical contribution.
author_followups: Continued Google Research automatic video editing and instructional-video tooling by Chi and Essa
Irfan Essa's broader generative video and content-creation research portfolio
notable_citers: Automatic video editing and editing-style-transfer follow-up work

title: Decorating Your Own Bedroom: Locally Controlling Image Generation with Generative Adversarial Networks
lineage_note: Proposes LoGAN for local, object-level control of GAN image generation (removing/inserting/moving furniture in synthesized rooms) by Chen Zhang, Yinghao Xu, and Yujun Shen — core members of the GenForce group known for InterFaceGAN and SeFa. The local-control idea is continued in the same circle's later controllable-synthesis work such as LinkGAN (linking GAN latents to pixels, CVPR 2023). Yujun Shen and Yinghao Xu are prolific, well-cited GAN-interpretation researchers, giving this notable lineage; exact S2 citation count for this specific paper was not confirmed.
author_followups: LinkGAN: Linking GAN Latents to Pixels for Controllable Image Synthesis (2023) — same GenForce lineage of spatially-controllable generation
InterFaceGAN / SeFa and broader GAN latent-space editing work by Yujun Shen and collaborators
notable_citers: GAN local image editing literature (e.g., latent-space local editing and parameter-space editing methods)

title: MorphGAN: Controllable One-Shot Face Synthesis
lineage_note: An Apple-internship work (published at BMVC 2021 as 'MorphGAN: One-Shot Face Synthesis GAN for Detecting Recognition Bias') by lead author Nataniel Ruiz, who shortly after went on to lead DreamBooth (CVPR 2023, Honorable Mention) — the landmark and now-standard method for subject-driven personalization of text-to-image diffusion models. MorphGAN is an early signal of Ruiz's trajectory in controllable, identity-preserving generative synthesis.
author_followups: DreamBooth: Fine Tuning Text-to-Image Diffusion Models for Subject-Driven Generation (Nataniel Ruiz et al., CVPR 2023) — landmark personalization method, CVPR 2023 Honorable Mention
HyperDreamBooth (Ruiz et al., CVPR 2024) — fast personalization follow-up
notable_citers: Multi-attributed Face Synthesis for One-Shot Deep Face Recognition (Springer)

title: GaussiGAN: Controllable Image Synthesis with 3D Gaussians from Unposed Silhouettes
lineage_note: A Brown/Adobe BMVC 2021 paper learning coarse 3D object representations as a small set of 3D Gaussians from unposed 2D mask supervision — conceptually prefiguring the 3D-Gaussian-as-primitive idea that later exploded with 3D Gaussian Splatting. Co-author Aaron Gokaslan went on to high-impact open-data/open-model work (OpenWebText, CommonCanvas), earning a Mozilla Rise25 honor; senior author James Tompkin's lab continued in controllable 3D generative modeling.
author_followups: CommonCanvas: An Open Diffusion Model Trained with Creative-Commons Images (Aaron Gokaslan et al., 2023)
OpenWebText dataset (Gokaslan & Cohen) — widely used GPT-2-replication corpus

title: Learning Physically-based Face Material and Lighting Decompositions
later_venue: Computational Visual Media (CVM) 2022/2024 — journal version 'Learning physically based material and lighting decompositions for face editing'
lineage_note: A Brown Visual Computing workshop abstract (AICC@CVPR'21) that matured into a journal paper in Computational Visual Media (8 citations, code released as brownvc/phaced). It sits in a research line on inverse-rendering and relighting of faces; co-author Vikas Thamizharasan went on to more visible graphics work including VecFusion (CVPR 2024 Highlight) and GEM3D (SIGGRAPH 2024), and the Tompkin lab continued toward controllable relighting and Gaussian head relighting.
author_followups: VecFusion: Vector Font Generation with Diffusion (Vikas Thamizharasan et al., CVPR 2024 Highlight)
GEM3D: Generative Medial Abstractions for 3D Shape Synthesis (SIGGRAPH 2024)
Physically Controllable Relighting of Photographs / GSHeadRelight (later Tompkin-lab relighting work)

title: Few-shot Font Generation with Localized Style Representations and Factorization
later_venue: AAAI 2021 (extended to IEEE TPAMI as 'Few-shot Font Generation with Weakly Supervised Localized Representations')
lineage_note: LF-Font, a well-cited (111 citations, 20 influential) AAAI 2021 NAVER/Clova paper that became a standard baseline in the few-shot font-generation subfield, spawning many follow-ups (MX-Font, Diff-Font, DA-Font, etc.) and a TPAMI journal extension by the same authors. Co-author Sanghyuk Chun is a prominent NAVER AI researcher (ReLabelImageNet, ProbVLM, dataset/representation-learning work).

title: Regularizing Generative Adversarial Networks under Limited Data
lineage_note: Introduces LeCam regularization (a LeCam-divergence-based loss), one of the most influential techniques (177 citations, 29 influential) for stabilizing GAN training on limited data. It was adopted as a default ingredient in major subsequent generative models including StyleGAN-XL and Projected GANs, and complements differentiable-augmentation lines (ADA, DiffAugment). Google authors Lu Jiang and Ming-Hsuan Yang are highly prominent in generative/video modeling.
author_followups: Later large-scale generative/video work by Lu Jiang (e.g., text-to-video research at Google)
notable_citers: StyleGAN-XL: Scaling StyleGAN to Large Diverse Datasets (Sauer et al., SIGGRAPH 2022)

numerous limited-data / data-efficient GAN papers

title: Exploiting Spatial Dimensions of Latent in GAN for Real-time Image Editing
lineage_note: StyleMapGAN (168 citations, 25 influential), a NAVER AI Lab CVPR 2021 paper replacing the vector latent + AdaIN of StyleGAN with a spatial 'style map' for fast, accurate encoder-based real-image editing — an influential idea in the GAN-inversion / latent-editing line. Led by the StarGAN/StarGAN v2 team: senior author Yunjey Choi (~9k+ citations) authored the landmark StarGAN and StarGAN v2, and the group continued shipping notable generative models at NAVER.
author_followups: StarGAN v2: Diverse Image Synthesis for Multiple Domains (Yunjey Choi et al., CVPR 2020) — landmark (same senior authors)

Co-authors became leading figures: Yujun Shen (Ant Research) and Bolei Zhou are prominent in generative vision.

lineage_note: AI4CC 2022 Best Paper introducing the FixNoise strategy to preserve disentangled source features during StyleGAN transfer learning. The same KAIST authors (Dongyeun Lee, Junmo Kim, et al., adding Jaejun Yoo) extended it into a full CVPR 2023 paper on controllable domain translation, making this workshop paper a direct precursor to a main-conference publication. The S2 citation count of 2 reflects only this early arXiv stub; the CVPR 2023 extension carries the impact.

lineage_note: AI4CC 2022 Best Paper Runner-up proposing Ray-marching in Camera Space (RiCS), a 2D self-occlusion map for harmonizing inserted 3D/volumetric objects (human image harmonization with coherent shading/shadows). Notable for its author pedigree: Ruben Villegas, Jimei Yang, Duygu Ceylan, Xin Sun (Adobe/Google) and Honglak Lee (Michigan/LG AI), with first author Yunseok Jang going on to CVPR 2025 work on mobile agents. title: Overparameterization Improves StyleGAN Inversion
lineage_note: AI4CC 2022 Best Poster showing that heavily overparameterizing the StyleGAN latent space yields near-perfect inversion/reconstruction while retaining editability, without encoders. The same Universite Laval group (Yohan Poirier-Ginter, Jean-Francois Lalonde) followed up with the well-regarded CVPR 2023 paper 'Robust Unsupervised StyleGAN Image Restoration' and later diffusion-based radiance-field relighting work, making this poster an early step in a productive StyleGAN-restoration / relighting line.

title: Zero-Shot Text-Guided Object Generation with Dream Fields
lineage_note: Landmark lineage. Dream Fields (Jain, Mildenhall, Barron, Abbeel, Poole) pioneered zero-shot text-to-3D by optimizing a NeRF so renders score highly under CLIP - the foundational CLIP-guided text-to-3D method. The same core authors (Poole, Jain, Barron, Mildenhall) then produced DreamFusion (2022), which introduced Score Distillation Sampling and ignited the entire text-to-3D explosion. ~657 citations and growing.
author_followups: DreamFusion: Text-to-3D using 2D Diffusion (Poole, Jain, Barron, Mildenhall, 2022) - landmark follow-up introducing Score Distillation Sampling (SDS)

title: SeCGAN: Parallel Conditional Generative Adversarial Networks for Face Editing via Semantic Consistency
lineage_note: A semantic-consistency cGAN for mask-free face editing. The senior authors are prominent computer-vision researchers — Tae-Kyun Kim (Imperial College London / KAIST) and Binod Bhattarai (then UCL, now lecturer at University of Aberdeen) — whose broader GAN-for-faces and medical-imaging GAN lines are well known, but this specific paper saw only modest direct uptake.
author_followups: Binod Bhattarai and Tae-Kyun Kim continued work on GAN-based face/identity synthesis (e.g., 3D Morphable Model-based photorealistic face generation) and Bhattarai's later GANs-for-medical-imaging research

title: Video-ReTime: Learning Temporally Varying Speediness for Time Remapping
lineage_note: Self-supervised learning of per-frame 'speediness' for video time-remapping, by Simon Jenni (Adobe Research) with Markus Woodson and Fabian Caba Heilbron. Jenni is an established self-supervised video-representation researcher (Senior Research Scientist at Adobe); this workshop paper sits within his broader, more-cited line on temporal self-supervision rather than being a high-impact node itself.
author_followups: Simon Jenni's self-supervised video work: 'Video Representation Learning by Recognizing Temporal Transformations', 'Time-Equivariant Contrastive Video Representation Learning' (ICCV 2021 oral), and 'Audio-Visual Contrastive Learning with Temporal Self-Supervision'

title: RewriteNet: Reliable Scene Text Editing with Implicit Decomposition of Text Contents and Styles
lineage_note: The most-cited paper in this set. From the Naver Clova OCR group (incl. Gayoung Lee and Sungrae Park), it decomposes scene-text images into content vs. style for realistic text editing. It is an early node in the scene-text-editing (STE) line that subsequent diffusion-based STE methods (e.g., 'Brush Your Text') build upon and cite.
author_followups: Naver Clova's continued scene-text-recognition and synthesis research; the same team's work on text image generation and OCR datasets
notable_citers: Brush Your Text: Synthesize Any Scene Text on Images via Diffusion Model

title: SaiNet: Inpainting behind objects using geometrically meaningful masks
lineage_note: Stereo-aware inpainting behind objects using generative networks, from the University of Surrey group (Violeta Menendez Gonzalez, Andrew Gilbert, Simon Hadfield) with BBC R&D collaborators. Ordinary impact; part of the group's broader novel-view-synthesis / scene-editing research but not a notable precursor.
author_followups: Surrey CVSSP group work on novel-view synthesis and scene editing by Menendez Gonzalez, Gilbert and Hadfield

title: LayoutBERT: Masked Language Layout Model for Object Insertion
lineage_note: A BERT-style masked-layout model for object insertion, from Adobe authors (Kerem Turgutlu, Sanat Sharma, Jayant Kumar). Fits the broader transformer-for-layout-generation trend (LayoutTransformer / BLT era) but had limited direct citation impact.
author_followups: Adobe authors' subsequent applied ML work on layout and content generation

title: Improved Image Generation via Sparse Modeling
lineage_note: Despite a near-zero citation count, this has the strongest lineage: it is early work by Roy Ganz with Michael Elad (Technion), a foundational figure in sparse coding/dictionary learning. Ganz went on to high-impact perceptually-aligned-gradients work — CLIPAG (WACV 2024) and Energy-Based CLIP text-to-image — and is now an Applied Scientist at AWS AI Labs. The paper's idea of imposing sparsity on GAN activations connects classical CSC/ML-CSC theory to deep generative modeling.

title: On Conditioning the Input Noise for Controlled Image Generation with Diffusion Models
lineage_note: Early (May 2022) diffusion-conditioning abstract from Adobe MDSR and IIT-Hyderabad exploring conditioning the input noise rather than the inference step. The senior Adobe authors (Surgan Jandial, Balaji Krishnamurthy) continued in controllable/exemplar image editing, later publishing REEDIT: Multimodal Exemplar-Based Image Editing (WACV 2025); the work sits in the pre-ControlNet wave of controllable diffusion generation but did not itself become a landmark.

lineage_note: Runway ML paper (code: runwayml/guided-inpainting) on two-stream keyframe propagation for video inpainting/editing, by Patrick Esser, Peter Michael and Soumyadip Sengupta. Esser is a landmark figure: co-author of High-Resolution Image Synthesis with Latent Diffusion Models (the core of Stable Diffusion) and lead applied researcher at Runway on the Gen-1/Gen-2 structure-and-content video models and Stable Diffusion 3. This 2022 keyframe-propagation/guided-inpainting line directly foreshadows Runway's Gen-1 video editing system.

title: Diverse Video Generation from a Single Video
lineage_note: AI4CC version of the VGPNN work (full paper 'Diverse Generation from a Single Video Made Possible', arXiv 2109.08591, ECCV 2022) from the Weizmann group: Niv Haim, Ben Feinstein, Niv Granot, Assaf Shocher, Shai Bagon, Tali Dekel and Michal Irani. It revived non-parametric space-time patch-nearest-neighbor generation and beat single-video GANs, paired with the group's image counterpart 'Drop the GAN' (CVPR 2022). The senior authors went on to landmark generative/editing work: Tali Dekel and Michal Irani co-authored Text2LIVE (ECCV 2022) and Lumiere (Google, SIGGRAPH Asia 2024); Assaf Shocher is a prominent single-image/internal-learning researcher.

title: Neural Volumetric Object Selection
lineage_note: CVPR 2022 paper (UIUC + Adobe Research: Zhongzheng 'Jason' Ren, Aseem Agarwala, Bryan Russell, Alexander Schwing, Oliver Wang) doing scribble-based 3D object selection/segmentation in NeRF and multi-plane-image representations. It is one of the early entries in the now-active NeRF/3D-segmentation line that includes Semantic-NeRF, ISRF, SPIn-NeRF and later 3D Gaussian Splatting segmentation. First author Ren joins UNC Chapel Hill as faculty (2026); advisor Schwing is a well-known vision researcher.

lineage_note: CVPR 2022 Google paper (Yang Zhao, Yu-Chuan Su, Chun-Te Chu, Yandong Li, Marius Renn, Yukun Zhu, Changyou Chen, Xuhui Jia) splitting face restoration into generation vs. reconstruction and introducing an identity-preservation metric, critiquing GFPGAN/GPEN identity drift. The same Google team followed with 'Towards Authentic Face Restoration with Iterative Diffusion Models and Beyond' (ICCV 2023), moving the line into diffusion-based blind face restoration alongside DR2/DiffBIR/PGDiff. It sits in the lineage that fed NVIDIA's later, much more prominent diffusion-representation work (e.g., ODISE), and lead author Jiteng Mu went on to ActorsNeRF (ICCV 2023). lineage_note: By Min-Gyu Park, Ju-Mi Kang, Je Woo Kim, and Ju Hong Yoon at Korea Electronics Technology Institute (KETI), this reconstructs full 3D humans from a single image via implicit re-projection (depth-map composition) networks. It sits in the active monocular 3D human digitization line (PIFu/PIFuHD/ICON family) but did not itself spawn a widely recognized follow-on; an ordinary contribution in a crowded area.

lineage_note: By Gongbo Sun (Beijing National Day School) and Ming Zhang (Peking University), this introduced RubbingGAN and the first rubbing-restoration dataset (collected from the Zhang Menglong Bei stele) to repair incomplete Chinese inscription rubbings. lineage_note: By Jurijs Nazarovs (UW-Madison, Statistics) and Zhichun Huang (CMU), this frames frame interpolation/animation as a continuous Warping Neural ODE producing a vector field for smooth in-betweening. Nazarovs has a substantial Neural-ODE / Bayesian deep-learning publication record (NeurIPS/CVPR), placing this in a credible methodological lineage though this specific paper has no famous direct successor identified.

lineage_note: By Ammar Ul Hassan Muhammad and Jaeyoung Choi (Soongsil University), FontNet learns a font-style embedding where distance reflects font similarity and translates glyphs into seen/unseen styles. It is one node in the author's sustained few-shot font-synthesis program — followed by 'Learning Font-Style Space using a Style-Guided Discriminator' (ESWA 2023), 'Exploiting mixing regularization for truly unsupervised font synthesis' (Pattern Recognition Letters 2023), and Patch-Font (2025) — and relates to the SKFont Korean font generator.

Its highly prolific senior authors (Timofte, Danelljan, Van Gool) went on to extensive super-resolution/diffusion work, though this specific paper is a solid contribution rather than a landmark.

lineage_note: Landmark-lineage 3D-aware GAN that fused an SDF-based 3D representation with a StyleGAN2 generator for high-resolution, view-consistent face/geometry generation; ~400 citations and 54 highly-influential, making it a canonical reference alongside EG3D/StyleNeRF/pi-GAN in the 3D-aware generation era. Co-authored by DeepSDF creator Jeong Joon Park (now a Michigan professor working on large-scale 3D scene generation and DiffusionPDE, NeurIPS 2024) and senior Adobe researcher Eli Shechtman; UW's Ira Kemelmacher-Shlizerman lab. It directly seeded the SDF-based 3D generative thread (e.g., SDF-StyleGAN).

title: Few-Shot Font Generation by Learning Fine-Grained Local Styles
lineage_note: FsFont, a CVPR 2022 paper from Baidu VIS (Jingdong Wang's group) that learns fine-grained local styles via cross-attention between content and reference glyphs; it became a standard baseline in the few-shot font generation (FFG) subfield and is repeatedly cited/compared against by later font-generation works (Patch-Font, MX-Font++, CD-Font, diffusion-based font generators). Citation count could not be retrieved exactly (Semantic Scholar API was rate-limiting); it is a well-cited domain baseline (order of ~100+) rather than a cross-field landmark.
author_followups: Jingdong Wang / Errui Ding (Baidu VIS) group: extensive subsequent generation and vision-foundation work
notable_citers: Patch-Font (Applied Sciences 2025)
MX-Font++: Mixture of Heterogeneous Aggregation Experts for Few-shot Font Generation
CD-Font: One-Shot Font Generation via Conditional Diffusion Model

title: Few-Shot Head Swapping in the Wild
lineage_note: HeSer, a CVPR 2022 Oral from Baidu / SCUT (Jingdong Wang, Errui Ding group) introducing Head2Head Aligner + Head2Scene Blender for few-shot head swapping; it is the first prominent head-swapping (not just face-swapping) method and is the de-facto baseline cited by subsequent head/face-swap and talking-head/deepfake works. Citation count not retrievable exactly via S2 (rate-limited); given Oral status, open-sourced code, and press coverage it is a notable, well-cited reference (order of ~100+) but domain-specific rather than a broad landmark.
author_followups: Hang Zhou (talking-face/audio-visual generation line)
Jiaming Liu, Zhibin Hong, Errui Ding, Jingdong Wang (Baidu): continued face/head generation work
notable_citers: Subsequent face-swapping / head-swapping and deepfake generation papers (2022-2024)

title: Discrete Representations Strengthen Vision Transformer Robustness
lineage_note: ICLR 2022 paper showing that adding VQ discrete tokens to a ViT's input layer improves out-of-distribution robustness by up to 12% across seven ImageNet benchmarks. Strong author lineage: Carl Vondrick (Columbia) leads a prominent robustness/self-supervision group; Mostafa Dehghani went on to co-lead landmark scaling work (ViT-22B, Scaling Vision Transformers, PaLI) and Gemini-era research at Google DeepMind; Lu Jiang (later Google/ByteDance), Rahul Sukthankar and Irfan Essa are senior Google researchers. First author Chengzhi Mao (with Vondrick) produced a substantial adversarial-robustness line (e.g., 'Adversarial Attacks are Reversible with Natural Supervision', ICCV 2021). Exact citation count unavailable due to S2 rate-limiting, but it is a well-cited robustness reference and its authors are tied to landmark follow-on work.
author_followups: Mostafa Dehghani: ViT-22B / Scaling Vision Transformers / PaLI; Gemini-era research (Google DeepMind)
Chengzhi Mao & Carl Vondrick: Adversarial Attacks are Reversible with Natural Supervision (ICCV 2021) and further robustness work
Lu Jiang: subsequent generative/video work at Google and ByteDance
notable_citers: Understanding Adversarial Robustness of Vision Transformers via Cauchy Problem
Follow-on ViT robustness and vector-quantized tokenization literature

title: Generating Videos with Dynamics-aware Implicit Generative Adversarial Networks
lineage_note: DIGAN is a well-cited INR-based video GAN (ICLR 2022) that set SOTA FVD on UCF-101 and enabled long/non-autoregressive video synthesis. Lead author Sihyun Yu (KAIST, Jinwoo Shin's group) immediately followed it with the landmark PVDM (Video Probabilistic Diffusion Models in Projected Latent Space, CVPR 2023) and later CMD (ICLR 2024) — DIGAN is the GAN-era precursor to that influential latent-video-diffusion line.
author_followups: Sihyun Yu et al., 'Video Probabilistic Diffusion Models in Projected Latent Space' (PVDM), CVPR 2023
Sihyun Yu et al., 'Efficient Video Diffusion Models via Content-Frame Motion-Latent Decomposition' (CMD), ICLR 2024
notable_citers: StyleGAN-V (CVPR 2022)
Latent Video Diffusion Models for High-Fidelity Long Video Generation

title: SeamlessGAN: Self-Supervised Synthesis of Tileable Texture Maps
later_venue: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics (TVCG) 2022
lineage_note: A solid materials/graphics paper (published in IEEE TVCG) tackling tileable multi-map texture synthesis. Authors Carlos Rodriguez-Pardo and Elena Garces are part of the SEDDI industrial-PhD line on materials/SVBRDF capture; the work is cited by later tileable-texture-via-diffusion efforts (e.g. MatFuse, Tiled Diffusion at CVPR 2025), making it a modest precursor to diffusion-based seamless texture generation.
author_followups: Carlos Rodriguez-Pardo / Elena Garces — subsequent SEDDI material-capture and SVBRDF/texture synthesis works (industrial PhD on Materials and Radiance Encoding)
notable_citers: MatFuse: Controllable Material Generation with Diffusion Models
Tiled Diffusion (CVPR 2025)

title: A-NeRF: Articulated Neural Radiance Fields for Learning Human Shape, Appearance, and Pose
lineage_note: A foundational articulated-human-NeRF paper (NeurIPS 2021, 300+ citations) that introduced skeleton-relative reparameterization for learning a body model from monocular video. It is a core precursor in the animatable/articulated NeRF avatar line; lead author Shih-Yang Su (with Helge Rhodin, Michael Zollhofer/Meta Reality Labs) directly followed it with DANBO (ECCV 2022), and it is widely cited across the human-NeRF / NeRF-avatar literature (HumanNeRF, Neural Body, etc.).
author_followups: Shih-Yang Su, Timur Bagautdinov, Helge Rhodin — 'DANBO: Disentangled Articulated Neural Body Representations via Graph Neural Networks', ECCV 2022
notable_citers: HumanNeRF
Animatable NeRF / Neural Body line of work on human radiance fields

title: V3GAN: Decomposing Background, Foreground and Motion for Video Generation
lineage_note: A three-branch GAN (foreground/background/motion) for unconditional video generation, published at BMVC 2021. Low citation impact (~4) and sits in the MoCoGAN/G3AN decomposition-based video-GAN lineage that was largely superseded by diffusion video models; no notable follow-on lineage identified for author Arti Keshari.
notable_citers: Cited within the MoCoGAN / G3AN decomposed video-generation literature

title: SVS: Adversarial refinement for sparse novel view synthesis
lineage_note: A sparse novel-view-synthesis method combining radiance fields with adversarial/perceptual refinement, published at BMVC 2022 (CVSSP Surrey + BBC R&D). Modest citation impact; lead author Violeta Menendez Gonzalez continued the line with ZeST-NeRF (BMVC 2023) and SaiNet as part of her PhD on generative virtual camera views for media production.
author_followups: Violeta Menendez Gonzalez et al., 'ZeST-NeRF: Using Temporal Aggregation for Zero-Shot Temporal NeRFs', BMVC 2023
Violeta Menendez Gonzalez et al., 'SaiNet: Stereo Aware Inpainting Behind Objects'

title: MetaDance: Few-shot Dancing Video Retargeting via Temporal-aware Meta-learning
lineage_note: An arXiv-only few-shot dancing-video-retargeting paper using temporal-aware meta-learning (near-zero S2 citations). Its significance is mainly as an early-career work in a star lineage: lead author Yuying Ge (HKU, advised by Ping Luo; with Yibing Song and Ruimao Zhang) went on to the landmark SEED multimodal-LLM series at Tencent ARC Lab ('Planting a SEED of Vision in Large Language Models', 'Making LLaMA SEE and Draw') and is now a staff researcher at XPENG on embodied AI.
author_followups: Yuying Ge et al., 'Planting a SEED of Vision in Large Language Models' (SEED), 2023
Yuying Ge et al., 'Making LLaMA SEE and Draw with SEED Tokenizer', 2023

title: Custom-Edit: Text-Guided Image Editing with Customized Diffusion Models
lineage_note: An AI4CC oral by the Seoul National University DSAIL group (Jooyoung Choi, Sungroh Yoon) with NAVER AI Lab (Yunjey Choi of StarGAN/StarGAN-v2 fame, Yunji Kim, Junho Kim). It combines DreamBooth/Custom Diffusion-style customization with Prompt-to-Prompt editing, and is regularly cited as a reference method in diffusion image-editing surveys. Solid but incremental rather than landmark.
author_followups: Jooyoung Choi and Sungroh Yoon's broader SNU diffusion line includes ILVR (ICCV 2021) and Perception-Prioritized (P2) weighting (CVPR 2022), both highly cited; Yunjey Choi is the first author of StarGAN (CVPR 2018) and StarGAN-v2 (CVPR 2020), landmark image-translation GANs
notable_citers: Diffusion Model-Based Image Editing: A Survey
Comprehensive exploration of diffusion models in image generation (Artificial Intelligence Review survey)
Image Editing with Diffusion Models: A Survey

title: SpaText: Spatio-Textual Representation for Controllable Image Generation
lineage_note: A landmark-lineage work: a Meta AI + Hebrew University/Reichman collaboration that introduced open-vocabulary region-annotated (segmentation-map + free-text) control for diffusion T2I, routinely grouped with ControlNet and GLIGEN as a foundational spatial/regional-control method. First author Omri Avrahami (also Blended Diffusion) went on to a string of well-known works, and several Meta co-authors are behind Make-A-Scene/Make-A-Video.
author_followups: Omri Avrahami: Break-A-Scene (SIGGRAPH Asia 2023), The Chosen One: Consistent Characters in T2I (SIGGRAPH 2024), Stable Flow and DiffUHaul (CVPR 2024) building directly on this controllable-generation line
Meta co-authors Oran Gafni/Yaniv Taigman (Make-A-Scene, Make-A-Video) and Devi Parikh
notable_citers: ControlNet (Adding Conditional Control to Text-to-Image Diffusion Models)
GLIGEN: Open-Set Grounded Text-to-Image Generation
InstanceDiffusion and numerous layout/region-controllable diffusion follow-ups

title: The Nuts and Bolts of Adopting Transformer in GANs
lineage_note: An empirical study of Transformers in GANs (proposing CNN-free STrans-G / STrans-D) from the CUHK-SenseTime / NTU S-Lab orbit. The S2 record for this exact title shows only ~4 citations because the canonical/extended version was renamed STransGAN: An Empirical Study on Transformer in GANs (which carries the real citation weight). Notable mainly for its prominent authors rather than this preprint's own impact; the transformer-GAN line (TransGAN, ViTGAN) was largely overtaken by diffusion.
author_followups: Co-authors Bolei Zhou (UCLA; CAM, Places, interpretable/steerable GANs) and Chen Change Loy (NTU; ESRGAN, many high-impact restoration/generation works) are very prominent; the work itself was superseded by the community's pivot to diffusion models
notable_citers: STransGAN (the authors' own extended version)
Transformer-GAN survey / review papers on deep generative models

title: Can We Find Strong Lottery Tickets in Generative Models?
lineage_note: First demonstration that strong lottery tickets (untrained sparse subnetworks, ~10% weights) exist in generative models, found via moment-matching scores; extends the Frankle-Carbin Lottery Ticket Hypothesis line into GANs. Published at AAAI 2023 (a notable venue) by Jaejun Yoo's UNIST group with Dongyoon Han (NAVER AI). Modest citation count but a clean, named contribution that the same authors built on.
author_followups: Same lead authors (Sangyeop Yeo, Jaejun Yoo, Dongyoon Han) followed with Nickel and Diming Your GAN: knowledge-distillation GAN compression (ECCV 2024)
notable_citers: A Survey of Lottery Ticket Hypothesis (arXiv 2024)
Generative-model pruning / efficient-GAN compression follow-ups

title: LPMM: Intuitive Pose Control for Neural Talking-Head Model via Landmark-Parameter Morphable Model
lineage_note: A NAVER WEBTOON AI workshop-style paper giving rig-like semantic pose/expression control over neural talking-head models via a landmark-parameter morphable model. Low citation count and ordinary direct impact, but it sits within NAVER Webtoon's well-known applied generative-avatar program (WebtoonMe, cartoon stylization) and several authors went on to more visible work.
author_followups: Namhyuk Ahn (WebtoonMe SIGGRAPH Asia 2022, DreamStyler) and Junsoo Lee continued NAVER Webtoon generative-creator tooling; Patrick Kwon moved to UCF CRCV and later co-authored story-visualization/video-generation work (DreamingComics)
notable_citers: Talking-head / portrait-animation and face-reenactment follow-up papers (limited)

title: LayoutDiffuse: Adapting Foundational Diffusion Models for Layout-to-Image Generation
lineage_note: An early-2023 method (USC/SJTU + AWS, incl. Mu Li, Tong He, Tianjun Xiao of d2l.ai/MXNet/GluonCV fame) that adapts pretrained foundational diffusion models to layout-to-image generation via a layout-attention adaptor and task-adaptive prompts. Nearly 100 citations and frequently referenced in the layout/region-controllable diffusion literature (alongside LayoutDiffusion, GLIGEN, InstanceDiffusion), making it a notable contributor to that subfield even though it remained an arXiv preprint.
author_followups: AWS co-authors Mu Li / Tong He / Tianjun Xiao are well known for the Dive into Deep Learning (d2l.ai) book and MXNet/GluonCV; lead author Jiaxin Cheng continued in controllable generation (note: the similarly named HiCo NeurIPS 2024 is a different Cheng at 360 AI, not an author follow-up)
notable_citers: Surveys and benchmarks on controllable/layout-to-image diffusion generation
Rethinking the Training and Evaluation of Rich-Context Layout-to-Image Generation; LoCo; STAY Diffusion

title: Putting People in Their Place: Affordance-Aware Human Insertion into Scenes
lineage_note: Co-authored by Tim Brooks (who invented InstructPix2Pix and went on to co-lead OpenAI's Sora video model, later joining Google DeepMind) and Alexei A. Efros (Berkeley). The work on conditional diffusion-based human/object insertion sits directly in the generative-video and controllable-image-synthesis lineage that produced Sora; it is a strong precursor work from this group.
author_followups: Tim Brooks co-led OpenAI Sora (2024 text-to-video world simulator)
InstructPix2Pix (Brooks, Holynski, Efros, CVPR 2023) — landmark instruction-based image editing
notable_citers: Subsequent affordance-aware and diffusion-based image composition / human-insertion works
CVPR/ICCV image inpainting and subject-insertion literature

Abdal — a prolific StyleGAN-editing and 3D-GAN researcher (Image2StyleGAN, StyleFlow) — went on to lead Gaussian Shell Maps (CVPR 2024), a notable 3D human generation method, continuing the editable-3D-avatar line this paper advances.

lineage_note: From KAIST (Jaegul Choo) and NAVER WEBTOON (Junsoo Lee). Extends Paint-by-Example with sketch conditioning for structure-aware composition. The same KAIST/NAVER WEBTOON collaboration produced DreamStyler (AAAI 2024), a notable text-to-image style-inversion method, situating this in an active diffusion-editing research line.

title: Exploring Gradient-based Multi-directional Controls in GANs
lineage_note: From ModiFace (L'Oreal's AR-beauty subsidiary) and University of Toronto, with founder Parham Aarabi as senior author. title: 'Tax-free' 3DMM Conditional Face Generation
later_venue: WACV 2024 (as 'Removing the Quality Tax in Controllable Face Generation')
lineage_note: This AI4CC workshop oral from James Tompkin's Brown University lab was the precursor to the same team's full conference paper, 'Removing the Quality Tax in Controllable Face Generation' (WACV 2024, authors Yiwen/Nick Huang, Zhiqiu Yu, Xinjie Yi, Yue Wang, James Tompkin), which closes the FID gap between 3DMM-conditioned face GANs and unconditional StyleGAN. title: CLIP-Actor: Text-Driven Recommendation and Stylization for Animating Human Meshes
lineage_note: Early (2022) zero-shot CLIP-driven text-to-mesh stylization work from Tae-Hyun Oh's KAIST lab; published at ECCV 2022. Lead author Kim Youwang continued this text-driven 3D synthesis line to the well-cited Paint-it (CVPR 2024, with Gerard Pons-Moll), a Score-Distillation-based PBR texture synthesis method. CLIP-Actor sits in the influential 2022 wave of CLIP-guided 3D stylization (alongside Text2Mesh/CLIPMesh).

title: High-fidelity 3D Human Digitization from Single 2K Resolution Images
lineage_note: A CVPR 2023 Highlight from Hae-Gon Jeon's GIST group that released the widely-used 2K2K dataset (2,050 high-resolution 3D human scans from 80 DSLR cameras) and a part-wise normal/depth pipeline for single-image human digitization. The released dataset and benchmark have become a standard reference for high-res clothed-human reconstruction, driving its relatively high (84) citation count and 11 influential citations.
notable_citers: Follow-on single-image clothed-human reconstruction and 3D Gaussian human avatar works that use the 2K2K dataset as benchmark/training data

title: Instance-Aware Image Completion
lineage_note: A low-citation (2) inpainting paper, but its co-author Minguk Kang (POSTECH, with Jaesik Park) is the lead author of the landmark GigaGAN ('Scaling up GANs for Text-to-Image Synthesis', CVPR 2023), and co-author Vibhav Vineet is a Microsoft Research scientist. The paper itself had little direct impact, but the author lineage is notable.
author_followups: GigaGAN: Scaling up GANs for Text-to-Image Synthesis (CVPR 2023) — lead author Minguk Kang, a landmark large-scale GAN text-to-image model

title: Context-Preserving Two-Stage Video Domain Translation for Portrait Stylization
lineage_note: A low-impact (1 citation) video portrait stylization paper, notable mainly for its heavyweight author list spanning KAIST (Sung Ju Hwang, Junmo Kim) and NAVER AI (Junho Kim, Yunji Kim, Hyunsu Kim), who are prolific in generative modeling. No clear direct follow-up lineage traces specifically from this workshop paper.

title: Visual prompt tuning for generative transfer learning
lineage_note: Highest-impact paper in this set (116 citations, CVPR 2023) from the Google Research team (Kihyuk Sohn, Huiwen Chang, Lu Jiang, Yuan Hao, Irfan Essa, Han Zhang). This token-space generative-transformer prompt-tuning work directly fed the team's landmark MaskGIT/Muse and StyleDrop line: Sohn, Chang, Jiang, Essa et al. authored Muse (ICML 2023) and StyleDrop (NeurIPS 2023), the latter applying the same parameter-efficient style-transfer-via-tuning idea to Muse. Clear precursor to famous Google generative models.
author_followups: Muse: Text-To-Image Generation via Masked Generative Transformers (ICML 2023)
