title: Matching-based Data Valuation for Generative Model
later_venue: ICLR 2025 (as 'GMValuator: Similarity-based Data Valuation for Generative Models')
lineage_note: Introduces GMValuator, billed as the first training-free, model-agnostic data-valuation method for generative models (similarity matching between generated and training images). The same arXiv id (2304.10701) was expanded and accepted to ICLR 2025 as 'GMValuator', so this AI4CC poster is the early version of a published conference paper. Co-author Yangsibo Huang subsequently became a Google DeepMind scientist and core contributor to Gemini 2.5 reasoning; senior author Xiaoxiao Li (UBC) leads a trustworthy-ML group. Exact citation count not retrievable (S2 rate-limited).
author_followups: GMValuator: Similarity-based Data Valuation for Generative Models (ICLR 2025) - direct successor
Yangsibo Huang -> Google DeepMind, core contributor to Gemini 2.5 / Gemini Thinking
continued data-valuation / trustworthy-ML work from Xiaoxiao Li's group
notable_citers: data-valuation and data-attribution survey/method papers (e.g. DValCards and follow-on generative-data-valuation work)

title: LocInv: Localization-aware Inversion for Text-Guided Image Editing
lineage_note: Uses segmentation/bounding-box priors to refine cross-attention maps and reduce editing leakage in Stable Diffusion text-guided editing. It is an incremental extension of the same group's NeurIPS 2023 'Dynamic Prompt Learning (DPL)' (Wang/van de Weijer), so it follows rather than precedes their more notable work. Senior author Joost van de Weijer (CVC Barcelona) is well established; the group's adjacent ECCV 2024 'ColorPeel' is a sibling line. Ordinary-tier workshop paper; citation count not retrievable (S2 rate-limited) but modest.
author_followups: Dynamic Prompt Learning: Addressing Cross-Attention Leakage (NeurIPS 2023) - precedes LocInv from same authors
ColorPeel: Color Prompt Learning with Diffusion Models (ECCV 2024, Butt/Wang/van de Weijer)
notable_citers: diffusion image-editing attribute/attention-leakage papers (e.g. 'Addressing Attribute Leakages in Diffusion-based Image Editing without Training')

title: Visual Style Prompting with Swapping Self-Attention
lineage_note: AI4CC 2024 Best Paper from Naver AI. A training-free style-transfer method that swaps key/value (keeps query) in late self-attention to inject reference style without fine-tuning. It became a standard baseline/building block in the tuning-free style-transfer literature alongside IP-Adapter, StyleAligned and InstantStyle (~58 S2 citations and growing). The same authors directly extended it into StyleKeeper (ICCV 2025). Senior author Yunjey Choi created StarGAN/StarGAN v2.
author_followups: StyleKeeper: Prevent Content Leakage using Negative Visual Query Guidance (ICCV 2025, arXiv 2510.06827) - direct successor by the same Naver authors
Yunjey Choi: StarGAN / StarGAN v2 (prior landmark work)
notable_citers: InstantStyle
InstantStyle-Plus
StyleAligned-related and IP-Adapter-based style methods
StyleRank style-transfer benchmark
numerous tuning-free style/attention-swapping diffusion papers

title: Automated Virtual Product Placement and Assessment in Images using Diffusion Models
lineage_note: An applied, three-stage industrial pipeline (language-guided segmentation -> few-shot fine-tuned Stable Diffusion inpainting -> alignment/quality filter) for advertising product placement, from the AWS Generative AI Innovation Center. Practical and well-engineered but a niche applied workshop paper with little academic follow-on lineage; appears in the object-placement / image-composition citation neighborhood (e.g. DiffPop). Exact citation count not retrievable (S2 rate-limited) but very low.
author_followups: Continued applied generative-AI work by AWS Generative AI Innovation Center scientists (Negin Sokhandan, Emmett Goodman)
notable_citers: object-placement / image-composition diffusion papers (e.g. DiffPop neighborhood)

title: VideoSwap: Customized Video Subject Swapping with Interactive Semantic Point Correspondence
lineage_note: Published at CVPR 2024 by Mike Zheng Shou's ShowLab (NUS) with Meta GenAI; it pioneered using a small set of interactive semantic point correspondences to swap video subjects while changing shape, an idea later echoed by subject/object-swapping video editors (HOI-Swap, DreamSwapV, DirectSwap). It is part of a prolific ShowLab/Meta video-generation cluster whose authors went on to more notable controllable-video work.
author_followups: MotionDirector (ECCV 2024, Oral) by overlapping ShowLab authors
DragAnything: Motion Control for Anything Using Entity Representation (ECCV 2024)
Lead author Yuchao Gu's subsequent long-context video world modeling / efficient generative modeling research and Meta GenAI internship
notable_citers: HOI-Swap (NeurIPS 2024)
DreamSwapV: Mask-guided Subject Swapping for Any Customized Video Editing
DirectSwap (video head swapping)

title: As-Plausible-As-Possible: Plausibility-Aware Mesh Deformation Using 2D Diffusion Priors
lineage_note: A CVPR 2024 paper from KAIST Visual AI Group (Minhyuk Sung) with Adobe Research that uses Score Distillation Sampling from a 2D diffusion model, via per-face Jacobians and a differentiable Poisson solve, to keep user-driven mesh deformations plausible. It is an early example of distilling 2D diffusion priors into explicit geometry editing; the same authors continued a strong geometry-plus-learning line at NeurIPS.
author_followups: Neural Pose Representation Learning for Generating and Transferring Non-Rigid Object Poses (NeurIPS 2024)
Neural Green's Functions (NeurIPS 2025)

title: ReasonPix2Pix: Instruction Reasoning Dataset for Advanced Image Editing
lineage_note: From the CUHK / CUHK-SenseTime Joint Lab group of Jiaqi Wang and Dahua Lin (the InternLM / InternVL / ShareGPT4V lineage), it contributes a reasoning-attentive instruction-editing dataset to push image editors toward handling implicit instructions, positioned against MGIE and SmartEdit. Citation count appears understated by the Semantic Scholar mirror; significance is more from the well-known author group than from measured impact so far.
author_followups: Dahua Lin and Jiaqi Wang's broader InternLM / InternVL multimodal LLM program
Reasoning to Edit: Hypothetical Instruction-Based Image Editing with Visual Reasoning (2025) continues the reasoning-edit theme
notable_citers: Reasoning-based instruction editing follow-ups (e.g., Reasoning to Edit, 2025)

title: MagicAnimate: Temporally Consistent Human Image Animation using Diffusion Model
lineage_note: A landmark CVPR 2024 paper (ShowLab NUS + ByteDance) that, alongside Animate Anyone, defined the diffusion reference-net / appearance-encoder + temporal-module recipe for human image animation, achieving >38% improvement over baselines on TikTok dancing. Its widely-used open-source release (magic-research/magic-animate) made it a de facto baseline, and it directly seeds the modern pose-driven animation lineage. Citation count from the Semantic Scholar mirror is conservative and almost certainly higher by 2026.
author_followups: Continued ShowLab human/character animation and 3D generation work
Co-authors' later ByteDance video-generation efforts
notable_citers: Champ: Controllable and Consistent Human Image Animation with 3D Parametric Guidance (ECCV 2024)
UniAnimate (Science China Information Sciences)
Wan-Animate: Unified Character Animation and Replacement

title: ToonAging: Face Re-Aging upon Artistic Portrait Style Transfer
lineage_note: A focused, single-stage method (Chung-Ang University group, Sanghyun Seo) that combines face re-aging with non-photorealistic portrait style transfer in one generative step by fusing aging and NPR latent vectors. Of niche/ordinary impact with low citations and no notable downstream lineage identified; the same group also works on cartoon-character hallucination detection.
author_followups: Cartoon Hallucinations Detection / VLM cartoon-character work by overlapping Chung-Ang University authors

title: Towards a Perceptual Evaluation Framework for Lighting Estimation
later_venue: International Journal of Computer Vision (IJCV) extended version: "Metrics for the Perceptual Evaluation of Lighting Estimation Algorithms"
lineage_note: A CVPR 2024 paper from Jean-Francois Lalonde's lab (Universite Laval) plus collaborators showing standard IQA metrics do not match human preference when judging relit scenes, and learning a combined metric that better tracks human perception, with released perceptual data and code. Notable as a methodological/evaluation contribution that was extended into a journal version (IJCV) and feeds the diffusion-relighting evaluation discussion (e.g., SpotLight).
author_followups: Extended journal version 'Metrics for the Perceptual Evaluation of Lighting Estimation Algorithms' (IJCV, by the same authors)
Lalonde lab's continued lighting-estimation and relighting work
notable_citers: SpotLight: Shadow-Guided Object Relighting via Diffusion

title: Customize Your Own Paired Data via Few-shot Way
lineage_note: An AI4CC 2024 poster from a ByteDance team (Bingchuan Li, Qian He, et al.) proposing a few-shot mechanism to learn paired image-editing effects from ~1% of the usual paired data via directional sample transformations on a diffusion pipeline. An ordinary industry workshop contribution with limited downstream citation footprint; the authors are part of ByteDance's generative-imaging group whose more visible work appears under product/model releases rather than this paper.
author_followups: Bingchuan Li and Qian He are part of ByteDance's image-generation/editing research line (related to ByteDance's diffusion editing and portrait/customization products), though no single landmark paper is cleanly attributable as a successor to this specific poster

title: EverLight: Indoor-Outdoor Editable HDR Lighting Estimation
lineage_note: Originally an ICCV 2023 paper from Jean-Francois Lalonde's lab (Universite Laval) with Adobe (Hold-Geoffroy, Eisenmann); the AI4CC 2024 listing is a re-presentation. It is the first single, editable HDR lighting-estimation method working seamlessly indoors and outdoors, a recognized milestone in the lighting-estimation lineage that the Laval group has led for a decade (Gardner, Hold-Geoffroy, Weber). It is cited by subsequent lighting-estimation work including diffusion-based methods such as LuxDiT (2025).
author_followups: Lalonde/Hold-Geoffroy group continued the lighting-estimation line with follow-on HDR/diffusion lighting-estimation papers (e.g., later editable and diffusion-based lighting estimation works); EverLight builds directly on the group's earlier ImmerseGAN (3DV 2022) and Weber et al. Editable Indoor Lighting (ECCV 2022)
notable_citers: LuxDiT: Lighting Estimation with Video Diffusion Transformer (2025)

title: NeRFFaceSpeech: One-shot Audio-driven 3D Talking Head Synthesis via Generative Prior
lineage_note: A CVPRW 2024 (AI4CC) paper from KAIST's Visual Media Lab (Junyong Noh group) combining a StyleGAN/3D generative prior with NeRF and parametric-face vertex dynamics for one-shot audio-driven 3D talking heads. A notable entry in the audio-driven NeRF talking-head line; it is tracked in community talking-head surveys (e.g., Awesome-Talking-Head-Synthesis) and cited by subsequent one-shot/3D talking-head work.
author_followups: Junyong Noh's KAIST lab continues producing 3D face/talking-head and generative avatar research; the student authors (Gihoon Kim, Kwanggyoon Seo, Sihun Cha) have follow-on face-animation/3D head papers in the same line
notable_citers: Indexed in Awesome-Talking-Head-Synthesis collection; cited by later audio-driven 3D talking-head synthesis papers (2024-2025)

title: FlexEControl: Flexible and Efficient Multimodal Control for Text-to-Image Generation
lineage_note: A controllable text-to-image method (weight-decomposition strategy cutting ~41% trainable params and ~30% memory vs Uni-ControlNet) that went on to be accepted at TMLR (Dec 2024). Notable mainly for its heavyweight author roster (Xin Eric Wang/UCSC, Mohit Bansal/UNC, Nanyun Peng/UCLA, Vicente Ordonez, plus Amazon researchers Robinson Piramuthu and Gunnar Sigurdsson), placing it in the controllable-generation lineage downstream of ControlNet/Uni-ControlNet.
author_followups: Lead author Xuehai He (UCSC, advised by Xin Eric Wang) has continued in multimodal generation/controllable T2I and vision-language work; senior authors Mohit Bansal, Nanyun Peng, and Xin Eric Wang have extensive subsequent landmark multimodal/controllable-generation output

title: VSTAR: Generative Temporal Nursing for Longer Dynamic Video Synthesis
lineage_note: Training-free method (Generative Temporal Nursing via Video Synopsis Prompting + Temporal Attention Regularization) for generating longer, more dynamic videos from pretrained T2V diffusion models; subsequently accepted at ICLR 2025 with official Bosch Research code. From Anna Khoreva's Bosch group (with Margret Keuper/Mannheim, Dan Zhang); cited by long-video and metamorphic-video-generation work such as ChronoMagic-Bench.
author_followups: First author Yumeng Li completed her Bosch/Mannheim PhD ('Improving Alignment and Controllability in GANs and Diffusion Models') and moved to Amazon as Applied Scientist; senior author Anna Khoreva leads generative-video/diffusion research at Bosch with a strong track record in semantic image synthesis and video editing
notable_citers: ChronoMagic-Bench (text-to-time-lapse video generation benchmark)

title: Pix2Gif: Motion-Guided Diffusion for GIF Generation
lineage_note: A motion-guided diffusion model casting image-to-GIF as text+motion-conditioned image translation; published at ECCV 2024. Its strongest lineage signal is its authorship: senior author Jianwei Yang and Jianfeng Gao of Microsoft Research, whose subsequent flagship work includes Magma: A Foundation Model for Multimodal AI Agents (2025, #1 on Hacker News) and the GLIP/Florence/SEEM/Set-of-Mark line, placing this workshop/conference paper within a highly influential MSR multimodal-foundation-model trajectory.
author_followups: Jianwei Yang -> Magma: A Foundation Model for Multimodal AI Agents (2025), plus GLIP, Florence, X-Decoder, SEEM, Semantic-SAM, Set-of-Mark; Jianfeng Gao is a senior MSR figure behind many landmark vision-language/foundation-model efforts
notable_citers: Cited in image-to-video / motion-controllable generation literature (ECCV 2024 proceedings ecosystem)

title: LOVECon: Text-driven Training-free Long Video Editing with ControlNet
lineage_note: A training-free baseline for long video editing built on Stable Diffusion + ControlNet, introducing cross-window attention for cross-frame coherence; later formally published in Science China Information Sciences (2025). From Zhijie Deng's lab at Shanghai Jiao Tong University, who is better known for efficient/accelerated diffusion and Bayesian deep learning, making this an early entry in that group's diffusion-video line rather than a field-defining work.
author_followups: Zhijie Deng's SJTU group continued on efficient/accelerated diffusion and video generation (e.g., consistency-model and fast-sampling diffusion work).

title: LoopDraw: a Loop-Based Autoregressive Model for Shape Synthesis and Editing
lineage_note: An autoregressive 3D shape model representing geometry as sequences of cross-sectional loops, from Rana Hanocka's Threedle/3DL lab at the University of Chicago (the group behind MeshCNN and Text2Mesh) with Greg Shakhnarovich (TTIC). Lead author Nam Anh Dinh went on to more prominent Threedle papers including 'Geometry in Style' (CVPR 2025) and 'LL3M: Large Language 3D Modelers' (2025), making this an early entry in a productive 3D-content-creation lineage.
author_followups: Nam Anh Dinh, Itai Lang, Hyunwoo Kim, Oded Stein, Rana Hanocka, 'Geometry in Style: 3D Stylization via Surface Normal Deformation', CVPR 2025
Nam Anh Dinh et al., 'LL3M: Large Language 3D Modelers' (2025)

title: Posterior Distillation Sampling
lineage_note: PDS is a named optimization method for parametric (NeRF/SVG) editing that extends the Score Distillation Sampling (SDS) / Delta Denoising Score (DDS) lineage by matching source/target stochastic latents to balance edit fidelity and identity preservation. The full paper appeared at CVPR 2024 (this is the AI4CC workshop instance), from Minhyuk Sung's KAIST Visual AI group, and PDS is regularly used as a baseline/method in subsequent score-distillation editing work.
author_followups: Minhyuk Sung's KAIST Visual AI Group continued a strong line of diffusion-based 3D generation/editing and score-distillation work after PDS.
notable_citers: Rethinking Score Distilling Sampling for 3D Edit and Generation (2025)
DreamSampler: Unifying Diffusion Sampling and Score Distillation for Image Manipulation (ECCV 2024)

title: Text Prompting for Multi-Concept Video Customization by Autoregressive Generation
lineage_note: A method for multi-concept customization of pretrained text-to-video models via autoregressive prompting toward the intersection of concept video manifolds. Notable mainly for its author roster (Google DeepMind / Google Research + UMD): Kihyuk Sohn (FixMatch, diffusion), Ruben Villegas and Mohammad Babaeizadeh (large-scale video generation, Phenaki/VideoPoet-era work), and Paul Voigtlaender. Lead author Divya Kothandaraman (UMD/Google) continued on personalized/controllable video generation (e.g., CamMimic, ImPoster).
author_followups: Divya Kothandaraman et al., 'CamMimic' zero-shot camera-motion personalized video generation (2025)
Divya Kothandaraman et al., 'ImPoster: Frequency Guidance for Subject Driven Action Transfer' (2024)
Co-authors Villegas/Babaeizadeh associated with Google's large-scale text-to-video generation efforts

title: The Lost Melody: Empirical Observations on Text-to-Video Generation From A Storytelling Perspective
lineage_note: An empirical/position paper (Andrew Shin, Keio; Yusuke Mori, Univ. of Tokyo; Kunitake Kaneko, Keio) examining limitations of current text-to-video models from a narrative/storytelling standpoint and proposing an evaluation framework. It is an observational contribution rather than a method, with no evident high-impact follow-up lineage; Yusuke Mori's broader research touches visual storytelling and NLP for games.
author_followups: Yusuke Mori continues work on visual storytelling and narrative-oriented generation/NLP.

title: Towards Safer AI Content Creation by Immunizing Text-to-image Models
later_venue: ECCV 2024 (as 'IMMA: Immunizing text-to-image Models against Malicious Adaptation')
lineage_note: This AI4CC best-paper runner-up by Amber Yijia Zheng and Raymond A. Yeh (Purdue) became 'IMMA' at ECCV 2024, proposing model-side immunization against malicious fine-tuning (LoRA/Textual-Inversion/DreamBooth) as an alternative to data-poisoning defenses like Glaze/Anti-DreamBooth. It directly seeded a landmark theoretical follow-up, 'Model Immunization from a Condition Number Perspective' (ICML 2025 Oral, ~1% acceptance), and the lab's safety/immunization line is tied to Yeh's 2026 Google Research Scholar Award.
author_followups: Amber Yijia Zheng, Raymond A. Yeh, 'IMMA: Immunizing text-to-image Models against Malicious Adaptation', ECCV 2024
Amber Yijia Zheng, Cedar Site Bai, Brian Bullins, Raymond A. Yeh, 'Model Immunization from a Condition Number Perspective', ICML 2025 (Oral)
Raymond A. Yeh earned a Google Research Scholar Award (2026), partly reflecting this safety/immunization research line
notable_citers: Concept-erasure / machine-unlearning works on text-to-image diffusion models (e.g., Reliable and Efficient Concept Erasure; Fine-Grained Erasure in T2I Foundation Models)

title: Reference-based Painterly Inpainting via Diffusion: Crossing the Wild Reference Domain Gap
lineage_note: A VITA-Group (UT Austin, Zhangyang Wang) x SHI Labs/Picsart AI Research paper on diffusion inpainting from cross-domain references. Lead author Dejia Xu went on to prominent 3D/4D generation work (4DGen, Diffusion4D at NeurIPS 2024), and senior author Humphrey Shi is a well-known generative-AI researcher (Versatile Diffusion, Picsart AI Research). The paper itself is a modest-impact contribution but sits in a highly productive lineage.
author_followups: Dejia Xu et al., 4DGen: Grounded 4D Content Generation (2023/24)
Hanwen Liang, Dejia Xu et al., Diffusion4D: Fast Spatial-temporal Consistent 4D Generation via Video Diffusion Models (NeurIPS 2024)
Humphrey Shi group, Versatile Diffusion and subsequent Picsart AI Research generative models
notable_citers: Painterly Image Harmonization using Diffusion Model

title: Spatial Steerability of GANs via Self-Supervision from Discriminator
lineage_note: Landmark lineage by author. First author Jianyuan Wang subsequently led VGGT: Visual Geometry Grounded Transformer, which won the CVPR 2025 Best Paper Award (and the related VGGSfM, CVPR 2024). Co-authors are major generative/3D-vision figures: Yujun Shen, Yinghao Xu, Ceyuan Yang (genforce), and advisor Bolei Zhou. This GAN-steerability work (later published in IJCV) is an early-career precursor to that highly influential trajectory.
author_followups: Jianyuan Wang et al., VGGSfM: Visual Geometry Grounded Deep Structure from Motion (CVPR 2024)
Jianyuan Wang et al., VGGT: Visual Geometry Grounded Transformer (CVPR 2025 Best Paper Award)
notable_citers: genforce GAN-control follow-ups
controllable image synthesis surveys

title: Paint-it: Text-to-Texture Synthesis via Deep Convolutional Texture Map Optimization and Physically-Based Rendering
lineage_note: Influential text-to-texture work from Gerard Pons-Moll's group (MPI Informatics / University of Tubingen) with Tae-Hyun Oh (POSTECH). Its DC-PBR re-parameterized SDS optimization for PBR texture maps became a reference point in the text-to-3D-texture line and is cited by major follow-ups including Meta 3D TextureGen and Make-A-Texture. The Pons-Moll/Oh team continued into high-profile 3D avatar/asset work (e.g., ELITE Gaussian head avatars at CVPR).
author_followups: Kim Youwang, Pons-Moll, Oh et al., ELITE: Efficient Gaussian Head Avatar (CVPR)
continued Pons-Moll group text-to-3D / texture and avatar research
notable_citers: Meta 3D TextureGen
Make-A-Texture: Fast Shape-Aware Texture Generation in 3 Seconds
TexDreamer (ICMR 2025)

title: InstructRL4Pix: Training Diffusion for Image Editing by Reinforcement Learning
lineage_note: An early attempt at using reinforcement learning (PPO with an attention-map reward) to fine-tune diffusion models for instruction-based image editing, from South China University of Technology. It is an ordinary, low-visibility workshop contribution with no strong subsequent lineage identified, though it sits within the broader and now-active RLHF-for-image-editing direction.

title: Seamless Human Motion Composition with Blended Positional Encodings
lineage_note: Best-poster-winning workshop version of FlowMDM (also at CVPR 2024), the first diffusion model for seamless human motion composition via Blended Positional Encodings; it introduced now-used Peak-Jerk / Area-Under-Jerk transition metrics and a widely adopted public codebase. The authors (German Barquero, Cristina Palmero, Sergio Escalera) continued into a strong human-motion lineage with multiple CVPR 2025 papers (MixerMDM, Rolling Prediction Models) and in2IN, making this a high-impact node in motion generation.
author_followups: Pablo Ruiz-Ponce, Barquero, Palmero et al., in2IN: Leveraging individual Information to Generate Human INteractions (2024)
MixerMDM: Learnable Composition of Human Motion Diffusion Models (CVPR 2025)
German Barquero et al., From Sparse Signal to Smooth Motion: Rolling Prediction Models (CVPR 2025)
notable_citers: subsequent long-form / compositional human motion diffusion works building on FlowMDM and its transition metrics

title: CustomText: Customized Textual Image Generation using Diffusion Models
lineage_note: A TCS Research (India) paper extending TextDiffuser with ControlNet for font-color/background/type control and small-text rendering. It is an ordinary, niche contribution in the visual-text-rendering subfield with no notable author follow-on lineage identified; it appears among the broader body of controllable text-in-image diffusion work (e.g., OrienText, UniGlyph) rather than as a driver of it.
notable_citers: controllable text-to-image / visual text synthesis follow-ups (e.g., OrienText, UniGlyph-type works)

title: The Curious Case of End Token: A Zero-Shot Disentangled Image Editing using CLIP
lineage_note: A small zero-shot CLIP editing note from Pinar Yanardag's lab (with Yusuf Dalva and Hidir Yesiltepe) at the AI4CC workshop. While the paper itself has few direct citations, the lineage is strong: this group's disentangled-editing line of work (NoiseCLR, CVPR 2024 Oral; GANTASTIC) led directly to FluxSpace (CVPR 2025) and the well-received LoRAShop (NeurIPS 2025 Spotlight, multi-concept editing in Flux/rectified-flow transformers). The end-token editing idea is part of this group's broader push toward training-free, disentangled semantic editing in diffusion/rectified-flow models.
author_followups: FluxSpace: Disentangled Semantic Editing in Rectified Flow Transformers (Dalva, Venkatesh, Yanardag — CVPR 2025)
LoRAShop: Training-Free Multi-Concept Image Generation and Editing with Rectified Flow Transformers (NeurIPS 2025 Spotlight)
NoiseCLR: A Contrastive Learning Approach for Unsupervised Discovery of Interpretable Directions in Diffusion Models (Dalva, Yanardag — CVPR 2024 Oral)
GANTASTIC: GAN-based Transfer of Interpretable Directions for Disentangled Image Editing in Text-to-Image Diffusion Models (2024)

title: SSR-Encoder: Encoding Selective Subject Representation for Subject-Driven Generation
lineage_note: An influential tuning-free subject-driven generation encoder (Token-to-Patch Aligner plus Detail-Preserving Subject Encoder) that became a standard reference/baseline in the personalized/customized image generation literature (sits alongside IP-Adapter, DreamBooth, Textual Inversion in survey/awesome lists). Published at CVPR 2024 with ~160 citations and an open PyTorch implementation. Several lead authors (notably the highly prolific Yiren Song, plus Yuxuan Zhang and Jiaming Liu) went on to a substantial body of subject-driven and personalization work (e.g., P3S-Diffusion point-supervised selective subject generation, ProcessPainter, DiffSim similarity metric).
author_followups: P3S-Diffusion: A Selective Subject-driven Generation Framework via Point Supervision (Yiren Song et al., 2024/2025)
ProcessPainter: Learning to draw from sequence data (Yiren Song et al.)
DiffSim: visual-similarity metric via denoising U-Net attention features (Yiren Song et al.)
notable_citers: Awesome-Personalized-Image-Generation curated reference lists
Subsequent subject-driven / customized diffusion encoders comparing against SSR-Encoder as a baseline

title: CamViG: Camera Aware Image-to-Video Generation with Multimodal Transformers
lineage_note: Low direct citation count, but very strong lineage: CamViG is from the Google Research VideoPoet team (Andrew Marmon, Grant Schindler, Jose Lezama, Dan Kondratyuk, Bryan Seybold, Irfan Essa) and adds explicit 3D camera-motion conditioning to a VideoPoet-style multimodal transformer for image-to-video generation. VideoPoet (Kondratyuk, Lezama, Schindler, Seybold, Essa et al.) won the ICML 2024 Best Paper Award, making CamViG a direct camera-control offshoot of a landmark video-generation model and an early entry in the now-major controllable/camera-aware video generation direction.
author_followups: VideoPoet: A Large Language Model for Zero-Shot Video Generation (Kondratyuk, Lezama, Schindler, Seybold, Essa et al. — ICML 2024 Best Paper)

title: ViVid-1-to-3: Novel View Synthesis with Video Diffusion Models
lineage_note: A training-free method from UBC Vision (Kwang Moo Yi's group, with Shweta Mahajan, Yuhe Jin, Erqun Dong) that reformulates single-image novel view synthesis as generating a smooth camera-orbit video, combining NVS diffusion models with video diffusion for view consistency. Accepted as a CVPR 2024 Highlight with ~70 citations; an influential bridge between video diffusion priors and 3D-consistent NVS that fed into the broader video-diffusion-for-3D / multi-view consistency line of work.

title: DL3DV-10K: A Large-Scale Scene Dataset for Deep Learning-based 3D Vision
lineage_note: A landmark large-scale real-scene dataset (10,510 videos, 51.2M frames across 65 POI categories) from the groups of Bedrich Benes and Aniket Bera. With ~411 citations it has become a standard benchmark and pretraining corpus for NeRF, generalizable NeRF, and 3D Gaussian Splatting research; the DL3DV-Benchmark (140 held-out scenes, NerfStudio/3DGS-compatible) is widely used to evaluate ZipNeRF, Mip-NeRF 360, Instant-NGP, 3DGS, etc., and the dataset is distributed via HuggingFace. Cited by numerous NVS/3DGS works (e.g., 3DGS-Enhancer with 2D diffusion priors).
notable_citers: 3DGS-Enhancer: Enhancing Unbounded 3D Gaussian Splatting with View-consistent 2D Diffusion Priors
NeRF Is a Valuable Assistant for 3D Gaussian Splatting (ICCV 2025)
Numerous generalizable NeRF / 3D Gaussian Splatting benchmarking papers

title: Temporally Consistent Object Editing in Videos using Extended Attention
later_venue: IEEE/CVF CVPR 2024 Workshop on AI for Content Creation (AI4CC)
lineage_note: A training-free localized video object-editing method (AmirHossein Zamani, Amir Aghdam, Tiberiu Popa, Eugene Belilovsky at Concordia/Mila) that replaces self-attention with an extended attention to enforce frame-to-frame consistency in a pre-trained inpainting diffusion model. Ordinary impact so far (~1 citation), with a public GitHub implementation; no major downstream lineage identified yet, though Belilovsky's group remains active in generative/representation learning.

title: ElasticDiffusion: Training-free Arbitrary Size Image Generation
lineage_note: CVPR 2024 paper (full title adds 'through Global-Local Content Separation') on training-free high-/arbitrary-resolution generation from a fixed-size pretrained diffusion model, positioned against MultiDiffusion and SDXL decoding; it drew press coverage (Rice/EurekAlert/TechXplore) and an actively used GitHub release. Lead author Moayed Haji-Ali went on to substantially more prominent generative work (Snap Research), making this an early step in a strong trajectory and the highest-impact paper in this set. NOTE: Semantic Scholar API was fully rate-limited (HTTP 429) across 25+ retries on both curl and WebFetch, so exact citation counts could not be verified and are left null rather than fabricated.
author_followups: AV-Link: Temporally-Aligned Diffusion Features for Cross-Modal Audio-Video Generation (ICCV 2025, Snap Research) — Haji-Ali, Ordonez et al.

title: ClickDiffusion: Harnessing LLMs for Interactive Precise Image Editing
lineage_note: A modest AI4CC workshop poster (very low direct citations, ~1 per a Semantic Scholar search snippet; live API rate-limited so unconfirmed) on combining LLMs with click-based UI for precise diffusion image editing. It is an early entry in a landmark author trajectory: Alec Helbling and Polo Chau (Georgia Tech) shortly after produced ConceptAttention (ICML 2025), a widely noted DiT-attention interpretation/segmentation method beating 15 zero-shot baselines. Tier reflects author/lineage trajectory, not this paper's own citation count.
author_followups: ConceptAttention: Diffusion Transformers Learn Highly Interpretable Features (ICML 2025) — Helbling, Chau et al., state-of-the-art zero-shot DiT interpretability/segmentation

title: EraseDraw: Learning to Insert Objects by Erasing Them from Images
lineage_note: AI4CC oral; also a full ECCV 2024 paper from Carl Vondrick's Columbia lab (arXiv version titled 'Learning to Draw Step-by-Step via Erasing Objects from Images'). It is a recognized member of the influential 'learn object insertion by inverting removal' lineage alongside ObjectDrop, Paint-by-Inpaint, Add-it and OmniPaint, and is used as a baseline by NVIDIA's Add-it. Co-authors went on to high-profile vision work. S2 API rate-limited so exact counts are null; note arXiv id 2409.00522 is the ECCV version, distinct from the project-PDF URL in the AI4CC entry.

Continued Vondrick-lab controllable-generation work (e.g., 'Controlling the World by Sleight of Hand')

ObjectDrop (object removal/insertion counterfactuals)

title: ICE-G: Image Conditional Editing of 3D Gaussian Splats
lineage_note: AI4CC poster on fast image-/mask-conditioned local color and texture editing of 3D Gaussian Splats (and NeRFs) via DINO-feature correspondence. Modest direct impact but a notable, well-connected author roster (Varun Jampani/Stability AI, Amit Raj/Google Research, Zsolt Kira/Georgia Tech, Muhammad Zubair Irshad/Toyota Research Institute); it appears in a 2025 survey of NeRF/3DGS editing. S2 API rate-limited so counts are null; Tier 2 driven by author prominence rather than measured citations.
author_followups: Senior authors Jampani (Stability AI 3D efforts, SV3D) and Amit Raj (DreamBooth3D, Google) continued prominent generative-3D research

title: TriLoRA: Integrating SVD for Advanced Style Personalization in Text-to-Image
lineage_note: arXiv/AI4CC poster integrating SVD into LoRA updates to reduce overfitting and stabilize style personalization for text-to-image (full title adds 'in Text-to-Image Generation'). No identifiable peer-reviewed venue, no notable follow-up work or well-known citers found; appears to be an ordinary, low-impact contribution within the crowded LoRA-variant space. S2 API rate-limited and no web citation signal found, so counts left null (likely very low).

title: LEAST: Local text-conditioned image style transfer
lineage_note: CVPR 2024 Workshop (CVFAD) paper from Adobe Media and Data Science Research benchmarking and improving localized (region-specific) text-conditioned style transfer. Low individual impact, but the same Adobe author group (Singh, Jandial, Java) is productive: it follows their Gatha (CVPRW 2023) and precedes ReEdit (WACV 2025) on exemplar-based diffusion editing. S2 API rate-limited so no reliable count obtained; left null (likely low, workshop paper).
author_followups: ReEdit: Multimodal Exemplar-Based Image Editing with Diffusion Models (WACV 2025) — Java, Singh, Jandial et al.
Gatha: Relational Loss for Enhancing Text-Based Style Transfer (CVPRW 2023) — same Adobe group (precursor)

title: My Body My Choice: Human-Centric Full-Body Anonymization
lineage_note: Third entry in Umur Ciftci & Ilke Demir's privacy/anonymization line, following 'My Face My Choice' (WACV 2023). The same Ciftci-Demir pairing co-created Intel's widely-publicized FakeCatcher deepfake detector (the 'biological signals' line). MBMC extends human-guided, context-aware anonymization from faces to full bodies via ControlNet/GAN diffusion modules.
author_followups: My Face My Choice: Privacy Enhancing Deepfakes for Social Media Anonymization (WACV 2023, Ciftci, Yuksek, Demir)
FakeCatcher: Detection of Synthetic Portrait Videos using Biological Signals (Ciftci & Demir, Intel Labs deepfake detector)

title: Stable Flow: Vital Layers for Training-Free Image Editing
lineage_note: Landmark-lineage authorship: Omri Avrahami (Blended Diffusion/Blended Latent Diffusion, SpaText, The Chosen One; now NVIDIA Research after Snap/Google/Pika), Or Patashnik (StyleGAN-NADA), Kfir Aberman (DreamBooth, Prompt-to-Prompt), Daniel Cohen-Or, Dani Lischinski, and Ohad Fried (SIGGRAPH 2019 Text-based Editing of Talking-head Video). Published at CVPR 2025 with an official Snap Research implementation. An early, influential method for training-free editing on Diffusion Transformers (FLUX/DiT), identifying 'vital layers' for attention injection plus an improved flow-model inversion.
author_followups: Blended Latent Diffusion (Avrahami et al.)
The Chosen One: Consistent Characters in Text-to-Image Diffusion Models (SIGGRAPH 2024)
DiffUHaul: Training-Free Object Dragging (SIGGRAPH Asia 2024)

title: Parallel Rescaling: Rebalancing Consistency Guidance for Personalized Diffusion Models
lineage_note: Recent (June 2025) work from Sangheum Hwang's group at Seoul National University of Science & Technology on improving DreamBooth/Textual-Inversion personalization by decomposing the consistency-guidance signal into parallel/orthogonal components relative to CFG. Part of the group's active personalized-diffusion line (e.g., APT: Adaptive Personalized Training; Delta-SVD compression). Ordinary impact so far; too recent for notable downstream lineage.
author_followups: APT: Adaptive Personalized Training for Diffusion Models with Limited Data (SeoulTech)
Delta-SVD: Efficient Compression for Personalized Text-to-Image Models

title: HyperGS: Hyperspectral 3D Gaussian Splatting
lineage_note: From University of Surrey's CVSSP (Thirgood, Mendez, Ling, Storey, Hadfield) and I3D Robotics; published at CVPR 2025. Introduces the first hyperspectral novel-view-synthesis benchmark and a latent 3DGS approach with a ~14dB accuracy gain. Already serving as a baseline/precursor for follow-up hyperspectral-Gaussian-splatting works.
notable_citers: Diffusion-Denoised Hyperspectral Gaussian Splatting (arXiv 2505.21890)
Hyperspectral Gaussian Splatting

title: Tiled Diffusion
lineage_note: Or Madar & Ohad Fried (Reichman University CV&G Lab); published at CVPR 2025. A training-free method for seamlessly tileable images / textures / 360-degree panoramas via flexible tiling and similarity constraints, with applications in game assets and texture creation. Senior author Ohad Fried has a strong content-creation lineage (Text-based Editing of Talking-head Video, SIGGRAPH 2019; co-author on Stable Flow).
author_followups: Stable Flow: Vital Layers for Training-Free Image Editing (CVPR 2025, Fried as co-author)

title: EOPose : Exemplar-based object reposing using Generalized Pose Correspondences
lineage_note: Adobe MDSR Lab (Rishabh Jain, Mayur Hemani, Mausoom Sarkar, Balaji Krishnamurthy) with IIT-Bombay's Sarthak Mehrotra. Clear lineage within Adobe's reposing/try-on line: ZFlow (ICCV 2021) and VGFlow: Visibility-Guided Flow Network for Human Reposing (CVPR 2023) precede it; EOPose generalizes reposing from humans to class-agnostic objects using unsupervised keypoint correspondences and an Objaverse-derived dataset.
author_followups: ZFlow: Gated Appearance Flow-based Virtual Try-on with 3D Priors (ICCV 2021)
VGFlow: Visibility Guided Flow Network for Human Reposing (CVPR 2023)

title: Don't Mesh with Me: Generating Constructive Solid Geometry Instead of Meshes by Fine-Tuning a Code-Generation LLM
lineage_note: Early work fine-tuning a code-generation LLM to emit CSG/CAD Python scripts (claimed first DL model trained to generate CSG geometry and first text-conditioned LLM completing 3D geometry). It sits at the front of the now-active LLM-for-CAD-program-generation line; the broader space rapidly produced higher-fidelity CAD-program-generation systems (e.g. LLM-driven program generation with B-Rep grounding, and design-procedure-based CAD-program data augmentation appearing in 2026).
author_followups: Group of Peter Eisert (Fraunhofer HHI / HU Berlin) and Alan Akbik (HU Berlin, creator of the Flair NLP library) — established senior authors whose labs continued CAD/geometry and LLM work; this CVPR-workshop poster reads as an early entry preceding the 2026 wave of LLM-driven CAD program generation papers
notable_citers: Towards High-Fidelity CAD Generation via LLM-Driven Program Generation and Text-Based B-Rep Primitive Grounding (2026)
Learning From Design Procedure To Generate CAD Programs for Data Augmentation (2026)

title: DreamBlend: Advancing Personalized Fine-tuning of Text-to-Image Diffusion Models
later_venue: WACV 2025 (IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision)
lineage_note: Amazon paper (Shwetha Ram, Tal Neiman, Qianli Feng, Son Tran, Trishul Chilimbi et al.) improving DreamBooth-style personalization by blending prompt fidelity from early checkpoints with subject fidelity from later ones via cross-attention-guided synthesis. It is a solid applied contribution in the crowded subject-personalization line (DreamBooth/Textual Inversion descendants) and was published at WACV 2025; impact is incremental rather than foundational.
author_followups: Authored within Amazon's applied-science group (Trishul Chilimbi is a senior Amazon AGI/science leader); represents Amazon Science productization of text-to-image personalization rather than a precursor to a single landmark paper

title: Harnessing the Power of Training-Free Techniques in Text-to-2D Generation for Text-to-3D Generation via Score Distillation Sampling
lineage_note: Analysis paper from Minsu Cho's POSTECH group studying how training-free 2D tricks (Classifier-Free Guidance, FreeU) transfer to Score Distillation Sampling for text-to-3D, characterizing CFG (object size vs surface smoothness) and FreeU (texture detail vs geometric error) trade-offs. It is a diagnostic/empirical contribution downstream of the DreamFusion/ProlificDreamer SDS lineage; useful but not a landmark-precursor.
author_followups: Minsu Cho leads an established POSTECH vision/correspondence group; Seungwook Kim is a recurring co-author. This is an incremental analysis within their broader 3D-generation and matching research rather than a precursor to a famous model.

title: VideoHandles: Editing 3D Object Compositions in Videos Using Video Generative Priors
lineage_note: Accepted to CVPR 2025 (not merely a workshop poster). First training-free method to edit 3D object composition in videos by lifting video-generative-model features to a shared 3D reconstruction, editing it, and reprojecting — temporally consistent, and shown to scale to CogVideoX. From the strong KAIST Visual-AI (Minhyuk Sung) + Adobe Research (Paul Guerrero, Duygu Ceylan, Chun-Hao Huang) collaboration that previously produced SALAD (ICCV 2023).
author_followups: Same KAIST+Adobe team behind SALAD (ICCV 2023, part-level latent diffusion for 3D shapes) and a sustained line of 3D generation/editing work; Guerrero and Ceylan are prolific Adobe research scientists, Sung leads the KAIST Visual AI Group
notable_citers: Cited within the 2025 video-editing / generative-prior literature (e.g. long/controllable video generation follow-ups)

title: MALT Diffusion: Memory-Augmented Latent Transformers for Any-Length Video Generation
lineage_note: Best-presentation oral. Long/any-length video diffusion via segment-level autoregressive generation with recurrent attention compressing history into a compact memory latent, giving robustness to error accumulation over minute-scale footage. Lead author Sihyun Yu (KAIST, then Google) is the first author of REPA (Representation Alignment for Generation, ICLR 2025 Oral) — one of the most influential recent diffusion-transformer training works — and the co-author roster (Agrim Gupta, José Lezama, David Ross, Irfan Essa, Jonathan Huang) is the Google team behind VideoPoet/W.A.L.T-class video models. Clear landmark-adjacent lineage.
author_followups: Sihyun Yu: REPA — 'Representation Alignment for Generation: Training Diffusion Transformers Is Easier Than You Think' (ICLR 2025 Oral, ~17x faster DiT training, FID 1.42), plus PVDM (latent video diffusion) and follow-on Representation Entanglement (2025)
Google co-authors (Kondratyuk, Lezama, Gupta, Ross, Huang, Essa) connected to VideoPoet and large-scale Google video-generation efforts
notable_citers: Long-video-generation follow-ups such as 'Mixture of Contexts for Long Video Generation' (2025)

title: Revisiting Diffusion Autoencoder Training for Image Reconstruction Quality
lineage_note: Directly continues the Diffusion Autoencoders (Diff-AE) line: senior author Supasorn Suwajanakorn (VISTEC) led the original Diff-AE (CVPR 2022), a landmark giving diffusion models a meaningful, decodable semantic latent. This paper diagnoses why DAEs reconstruct blurry images (linear-beta schedule over-weights high noise) and proposes a two-phase training scheme (autoencoder-style high-noise phase, then a low-noise-weighted schedule) to sharpen reconstructions. High-quality continuation within an influential lineage; the paper itself is incremental but lineage is strong.
author_followups: Supasorn Suwajanakorn: Diffusion Autoencoders (Diff-AE, CVPR 2022, highly cited) and the iconic 'Synthesizing Obama: learning lip-sync from audio' (SIGGRAPH 2017); this work refines his own Diff-AE framework
Co-authors at VISTEC (Thawatdamrongkit, Seripanitkarn) plus Pramook Khungurn (pixiv)
notable_citers: Cited within the diffusion-autoencoder / representation-learning thread descending from Diff-AE and Hierarchical Diffusion Autoencoders

title: VideoLifter: Lifting Videos to 3D with Fast Hierarchical Stereo Alignment
lineage_note: AI4CC 2025 Best Paper. It sits squarely in the high-impact VITA-Group (Zhangyang/Atlas Wang, Zhiwen Fan) + Meta + Stanford (Leonidas Guibas) feed-forward 3D reconstruction lineage, directly extending InstantSplat (Zhiwen Fan et al., arXiv 2403.20309, also adopted by NVIDIA as NVlabs/InstantSplat) and the NeurIPS 2024 Large Spatial Model to scalable video-to-3D via hierarchical stereo alignment, claiming >82% faster reconstruction over SOTA. First author Wenyan Cong received a CVPR workshop Best Paper award for it.
author_followups: InstantSplat: Sparse-view SfM-free Gaussian Splatting in Seconds (Zhiwen Fan et al., arXiv 2403.20309; adopted as NVlabs/InstantSplat) — precursor that VideoLifter scales to video
Large Spatial Model: End-to-end Unposed Images to Semantic 3D (Zhiwen Fan et al., NeurIPS 2024) — same group's feed-forward 3D line
notable_citers: Works building on InstantSplat / feed-forward Gaussian splatting and DUSt3R/MASt3R-style stereo for video-to-3D reconstruction

title: CamCo: Camera-Controllable 3D-Consistent Image-to-Video Generation
lineage_note: An NVIDIA + UT Austin paper (Dejia Xu, Weili Nie, Sifei Liu, Jan Kautz, Zhangyang Wang, Arash Vahdat) that introduced Plücker-coordinate camera conditioning plus epipolar-constraint attention for 3D-consistent camera control in image-to-video diffusion — a building block of the now-mainstream camera-controllable video generation line alongside CameraCtrl/MotionCtrl. It precedes the authors' broader 4D/video-diffusion trajectory (Dejia Xu's 4DGen, Diffusion4D at NeurIPS) and Arash Vahdat/Jan Kautz's NVIDIA video generation program.
author_followups: Diffusion4D (Dejia Xu et al., NeurIPS 2024) — 4D generation via video diffusion
4DGen: Grounded 4D Content Generation (Dejia Xu et al.) — earlier 4D line continued at NVIDIA
notable_citers: CamViG: Camera Aware Image-to-Video Generation with Multimodal Transformers
CamC2V: Context-aware Controllable Video Generation
Camera-control video diffusion follow-ups (CameraCtrl/MotionCtrl family)

title: HumanEdit: A High-Quality Human-Rewarded Dataset for Instruction-based Image Editing
lineage_note: A human-rewarded instruction-editing dataset (5,751 masked 1024x1024 pairs, 2,500+ annotation hours) released as a benchmark on Hugging Face. Notable for its author roster: Ling Yang (RPG-DiffusionMaster ICML 2024, IterComp/Rectified Diffusion at ICLR 2025; now Princeton postdoc), Xiangtai Li (Staff Research Scientist at TikTok/ByteDance), Hanwang Zhang, and Shuicheng Yan — a cluster behind several landmark diffusion/MLLM works. The dataset is positioned as a standard evaluation benchmark for instruction-based image editing.
author_followups: RPG-DiffusionMaster: Mastering Text-to-Image Diffusion (Ling Yang et al., ICML 2024)
IterComp, Rectified Diffusion (Ling Yang et al., ICLR 2025)
notable_citers: Instruction-based image-editing models and benchmarks adopting HumanEdit as an evaluation set

title: Kubrick: Multimodal Agent Collaborations for Synthetic Video Generation
lineage_note: A Purdue + Baidu USA (Liu He, Daniel Aliaga) framework using collaborating VLM agents (Director/Programmer/Reviewer) to write Blender scripts for physically-correct synthetic video, an early entry in the agentic/procedural-3D approach to text-to-video that contrasts with end-to-end diffusion. It is cited by related procedural-video work such as Scene Co-pilot, and connects to the same group's Refine-by-Align line.
author_followups: Refine-by-Align: Reference-Guided Artifacts Refinement through Semantic Alignment (related Liu He / Aliaga group work)
notable_citers: Scene Co-pilot: Procedural Text to Video Generation with Human in the Loop

title: LiftRefine: Progressively Refined View Synthesis from 3D Lifting with Volume-Triplane Representations
lineage_note: A VinAI Research + Trinity College Dublin paper (Tung Do, Anh Tuan Tran, Binh-Son Hua) on single/few-view 3D neural-field synthesis combining a volume-to-triplane reconstruction model with a diffusion refiner and iterative progressive refinement, evaluated on SRN-Cars and CO3D. It extends the HoloDiffusion / Viewset Diffusion / image-conditioned 3D generative-model line; solid contribution from an established generative-3D group but without (yet) a landmark downstream footprint.
author_followups: Ongoing VinAI generative 3D / diffusion work by Anh Tuan Tran and Binh-Son Hua
notable_citers: Image-conditioned 3D generative-model follow-ups (HoloDiffusion / Viewset Diffusion family)

title: Generating Animated Layouts as Structured Text Representations
lineage_note: A Seoul National University + SK Telecom paper introducing 'Animated Layout Generation' via a hierarchical Structured Text Representation and the VAKER (Video Ad maKER) pipeline for LLM-driven text-to-video advertisement generation. Industry-applied (SK Telecom) and within the active LLM-based layout-generation line (PosterLLaVa, PosterO, Parse-Then-Place), but an ordinary-impact, application-focused contribution with no established notable downstream lineage yet.
notable_citers: LLM-based content-aware/layout-generation works (PosterO, LLMs as Layout Designers)

title: DANTE-AD: Dual-Vision Attention Network for Long-Term Audio Description
lineage_note: From the University of Surrey group of Andrew Gilbert and Simon Hadfield, DANTE-AD proposes a dual-vision (frame + scene) transformer with sequential cross-attention for long-term movie audio description, positioned within the influential AutoAD line of work (AutoAD I/II/III from VGG Oxford). Despite being a workshop paper, it has already been picked up by the audio-description evaluation literature. Its significance is as a building block in the maturing automatic-AD field rather than a standalone landmark.
notable_citers: What You See is What You Ask: Evaluating Audio Descriptions (arXiv:2510.00808, 2025)

title: Towards Flim-Making Production Dialogue, Narration, Monologue Adaptive Moving Dubbing Benchmarks
lineage_note: Produced by Giant Network's AI Lab (Junjie Zheng, Zihao Chen, Chaofan Ding, Xinhan Di and colleagues), this TA-Dubbing benchmark proposal is part of a focused, active line of movie-dubbing research from the same team, which also includes DeepDubber-V1 (arXiv:2503.23660) and FlowDubber (ACM Multimedia 2025). It sits in a fast-growing emotion/style-adaptive dubbing subfield (EmoDubber, MM-MovieDubber). Notable as a benchmark-defining effort within that team's coherent research program, but low external citation so far.
author_followups: DeepDubber-V1 (arXiv:2503.23660, 2025)
FlowDubber (ACM Multimedia 2025)

title: Comparison Reveals Commonality: Customized Image Generation through Contrastive Inversion
lineage_note: From Junmo Kim's group at KAIST (with Hanbat National University), this work extends the Textual Inversion / DreamBooth personalization line by jointly learning auxiliary image-wise tokens via contrastive learning to disentangle a shared target concept without extra guidance. It is a recent (August 2025) entry in the crowded subject-driven generation area; too new to have accumulated citations, and it reads as an incremental-but-clean contribution rather than a precursor to a landmark.

title: HyperNVD: Accelerating Neural Video Decomposition via Hypernetworks
later_venue: CVPR 2025 (IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition)
lineage_note: By Maria Pilligua, Danna Xue, and Javier Vazquez-Corral (Computer Vision Center / UAB Barcelona), HyperNVD uses a VideoMAE-conditioned hypernetwork plus meta-learning to generate the weights of a compact INR-based layered video-decomposition model, sharply speeding up per-video training. It builds directly on the layered/INR video-editing lineage (Omnimatte, Hashing-NVD) and was accepted to the CVPR 2025 main conference, signaling solid quality; impact is still early.

title: Deep Geometric Moments Promote Shape Consistency in Text-to-3D Generation
later_venue: WACV 2025 (IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision)
lineage_note: From Arizona State University's Geometric Media Lab (Pavan Turaga, Utkarsh Nath, Rajeev Goel) with Intel Labs, this work (MT3D) injects deep geometric moments and depth control from a high-fidelity 3D object to combat viewpoint bias and the Janus problem in SDS-based text-to-3D, reporting a 38% lower Janus rate than HiFA. It is the most-cited paper in this batch and a recognized contribution in the text-to-3D/SDS lineage (DreamFusion, Magic3D, HiFA), though not itself a foundational landmark.
notable_citers: DreamCS: Geometry-Aware Text-to-3D Generation with Unpaired 3D Reward Supervision (arXiv:2506.09814, 2025)

title: Vectorized Region Based Brush Strokes for Artistic Rendering
lineage_note: By Jeripothula Prudviraj and Vikram Jamwal of TCS Research (India), this paper extends the team's own stroke-based painterly-rendering line; its direct predecessor, 'Sketch & Paint: Stroke-by-Stroke Evolution of Visual Artworks,' appeared at the ECCV 2024 Workshops (Springer). It contributes semantic region-guided vectorized stroke ordering for image-to-painting rendering. A solid, self-consistent research thread from an industry lab, but ordinary in broader impact terms.
author_followups: Sketch & Paint: Stroke-by-Stroke Evolution of Visual Artworks (ECCV 2024 Workshops) — predecessor by the same authors

title: GenSync: A Generalized Talking Head Framework for Audio-driven Multi-Subject Lip-Sync using 3D Gaussian Splatting
lineage_note: A student-led (UMass Amherst) workshop paper applying 3D Gaussian Splatting to multi-identity audio-driven talking heads, claiming 6.8x faster training via an identity/audio disentanglement module. Citations are very low (2) and it has remained an arXiv-only preprint with no top-venue follow-on or notable downstream adoption yet; lineage is ordinary so far.

title: Enhancing Creative Generation on Stable Diffusion-based Models
lineage_note: Introduces C3 (Creative Concept Catalyst), a training-free feature-amplification method to make Stable-Diffusion outputs more creative; published at CVPR 2025 (main conference) with code released. Notable for its senior authors from NAVER AI Lab (Gayoung Lee, Junho Kim — established generative-model researchers) and KAIST (Jaesik Choi); a solid, moderately cited contribution rather than a landmark.

title: NamedCurves: Learned Image Enhancement via Color Naming
lineage_note: Decomposes images into named colors and learns per-color Bezier tone curves fused by attention for global image enhancement; originally an ECCV 2024 paper that was subsequently extended to a TPAMI 2026 journal version (per the authors' official repo), a strong quality signal. Senior author Michael S. Brown is a highly prominent computational-photography/color researcher, lending the line notable standing despite modest raw citations.
author_followups: TPAMI 2026 journal extension of NamedCurves by the same authors (Serrano-Lozano, Herranz, Brown, Vazquez-Corral)

title: ScribbleLight: Single Image Indoor Relighting with Scribbles
lineage_note: A generative single-image indoor relighting method offering fine-grained scribble-based control of lighting, published at CVPR 2025 and the most-cited paper in this set (16 cites, 2 influential). Sits in the strong relighting lineage of senior author Anand Bhattad (StyLitGAN CVPR 2024, LumiNet 2024/25) with co-authors Roni Sengupta and Pieter Peers, making it a notable node in an active intrinsic-image/relighting research line.
author_followups: LumiNet: Latent Intrinsics Meets Diffusion Models for Indoor Scene Relighting (Bhattad et al., 2024/2025)

title: DiT-VTON: Diffusion Transformer Framework for Unified Multi-Category Virtual Try-On and Virtual Try-All with Integrated Image Editing
lineage_note: An Amazon (Karim Bouyarmane group) Diffusion-Transformer virtual try-on/try-all system extending try-on to arbitrary product categories (furniture, jewelry, accessories) with integrated editing. It is part of Amazon's productized VTON research lineage (Diffuse to Choose, DreamPaint, DEFT-VTON), but as a brand-new arXiv preprint it currently has zero citations and no independent downstream impact; industrial relevance is real but academic lineage is nascent.
author_followups: DEFT-VTON: Efficient Virtual Try-On with Consistent Generalised H-Transform (Xu, Li, Qiu, Han, Bouyarmane, CVPR 2025)

title: Is Concatenation Really All You Need? Efficient Concatenation-Based Pose Conditioning and Pose Control for Virtual Try On
lineage_note: Same Amazon/Bouyarmane try-on team; the arXiv version is titled 'Efficient Encoder-Free Pose Conditioning and Pose Control for Virtual Try-On' (arXiv:2509.20343). The title directly responds to CatVTON ('Concatenation Is All You Need for Virtual Try-On'), adding spatial pose concatenation and a mixed-mask training strategy to the pure-concatenation paradigm. Low citations (3) and arXiv-only; a modest incremental industrial contribution rather than a landmark.
author_followups: DiT-VTON (Li, Qiu, Han, Bouyarmane et al., 2025)
DEFT-VTON (CVPR 2025)
notable_citers: Builds directly on / responds to CatVTON: Concatenation Is All You Need for Virtual Try-On with Diffusion Models (Chong et al., 2024)

title: Art3D: Training-Free 3D Generation from Flat-Colored Illustration
lineage_note: A training-free method to lift flat-colored 2D illustrations into 3D using pretrained 2D priors plus a VLM-based realism check, introducing the Flat-2D benchmark. Its significance is mainly as a product of Srinath Sridhar's Brown IVL group, a prominent 3D/Gaussian-splatting lab; the group's adjacent line (e.g., UVGS at CVPR 2025) is the more notable downstream work.
author_followups: UVGS: Reimagining Unstructured 3D Gaussian Splatting using UV Mapping (CVPR 2025, Sridhar lab)
Sridhar lab line of 3D Gaussian / volumetric-video work (MANUS CVPR 2024, large multi-view video datasets)

title: 4K4DGen: Panoramic 4D Generation at 4K Resolution
lineage_note: First method to elevate a single panorama to an immersive 4D (4K, 360 degrees) experience via a Panoramic Denoiser plus Dynamic Panoramic Lifting into dynamic Gaussians; accepted as an ICLR 2025 Spotlight. It sits in a high-impact author cluster (Zhiwen Fan, Zhangyang Wang/VITA, Achuta Kadambi/UCLA, Zhengzhong Tu) that produced a whole lineage of 4D / panoramic scene-generation work, making it a clear precursor node in the panoramic-4D space.
author_followups: Feature4X: Bridging Any Monocular Video to 4D Agentic AI (CVPR 2025; shares Zhou, Xu, Fan, Wang, Kadambi)
DreamScene360 (panoramic text-to-3D Gaussian splatting; overlapping authors)
Broader VITA-group / Kadambi-lab 4D scene generation line
notable_citers: TiP4GEN: Text to Immersive Panorama 4D Scene Generation
ViewPoint: Panoramic Video Generation with Pretrained Diffusion Models

title: Is Your Text-to-Image Model Robust to Caption Noise?
lineage_note: Builds a dataset of VLM-generated captions and shows caption hallucination measurably degrades T2I fine-tuning, proposing VLM-confidence-based noise mitigation. Its weight comes largely from the senior author, Lu Jiang (ByteDance Intelligent Creation, part-time CMU professor, former lead of Google's VideoPoet), a prominent figure in the generative-video/image space; note the paper's authors do NOT appear on ByteDance's Seedream 2.0 author list, so no direct authorship link to Seedream should be claimed.
author_followups: Lu Jiang's ByteDance generative-AI program (VideoPoet -> ByteDance Intelligent Creation; broader Seed/Seedream-era T2I and video efforts at the same org)
Prior landmark video-gen work by the senior author: VideoPoet, MAGVIT (CVPR 2023 Highlight), W.A.L.T

title: Training-Free Sketch-Guided Diffusion with Latent Optimization
lineage_note: A training-free pipeline adding sketch conditioning to T2I models by optimizing the noisy latent against cross-attention maps, from the Aizawa lab at the University of Tokyo (lead Sandra Zhang Ding, with Jiafeng Mao). An ordinary but solid workshop contribution; it extends the established cross-attention / training-free layout-control line (e.g., Training-Free Layout Control with Cross-Attention Guidance) rather than founding a new one.
author_followups: Jiafeng Mao's continued training-free controllable-generation / layout-control work at UTokyo

title: InstructVTON: Optimal Auto-Masking and Natural-Language-Guided Interactive Style Control for Inpainting-Based Virtual Try-On
lineage_note: An Amazon (with UCLA/Duke collaborators) interactive virtual try-on system that uses VLMs plus segmentation for automatic mask generation and free-text styling, removing the need for hand-drawn masks. Its lineage is industrial/applied: it sits within Amazon's ongoing virtual-try-on research program (e.g., DEFT-VTON) led in part by Karim Bouyarmane, rather than being an academic precursor.
author_followups: DEFT-VTON: Efficient Virtual Try-On with Consistent Generalised H-Transform (Amazon)
Amazon's broader inpainting-based virtual-try-on product/research line (Bouyarmane, Asadi et al.)

title: Progressive Prompt Detailing for Improved Alignment in Text-to-Image Generative Models
lineage_note: Proposes SCoPE, a training-free, plug-and-play method that decomposes a long prompt into coarse-to-fine sub-prompts and interpolates their embeddings during inference to boost prompt alignment (reported +8 VQA over SD baselines on GenAI-Bench). Notable mainly via senior author Deepti Ghadiyaram (Boston University assistant professor and Runway MTS, ex-Meta/FAIR), whose group works on interpreting and steering generative representations.
author_followups: Ghadiyaram group's interpretability/alignment line (e.g., 'Interpreting and Leveraging Generative Representations'; follow-on T2I misalignment work such as Noise Projection)
Runway-affiliated generative-model alignment research

title: LumiNet: Latent Intrinsics Meets Diffusion Models for Indoor Scene Relighting
lineage_note: Builds directly on senior author Anand Bhattad's StyLitGAN line (CVPR 2024) and his 'StyleGAN knows Normal, Depth, Albedo' work, marrying latent-intrinsic relighting with diffusion ControlNet. Bhattad is now a TTIC faculty member and a recognized voice in intrinsic-image/generative-relighting research, so LumiNet sits within a notable and active relighting lineage rather than being a one-off.
author_followups: StyLitGAN: Image-Based Relighting via Latent Control (Bhattad, CVPR 2024) — direct precursor whose data-curation strategy LumiNet reuses
StyleGAN knows Normal, Depth, Albedo, and More (Bhattad et al.) — earlier intrinsics-from-generators line by the same senior author
notable_citers: Subsequent diffusion-based scene-relighting and intrinsic-decomposition papers (2025) citing it as a latent-intrinsics + diffusion baseline

title: Perceptually Accurate 3D Talking Head Generation: New Definitions, Speech-Mesh Representation, and Evaluation Metrics
lineage_note: A CVPR 2025 Highlight from Tae-Hyun Oh's group (KAIST/POSTECH, KRAFTON), introducing a speech-mesh synchronized representation and new perceptual metrics (Temporal Synchronization, Lip Readability, Expressiveness) for 3D talking heads. Oh is an established audio-visual-learning researcher (ex-MIT CSAIL), and the group has rapid follow-ups (e.g., 'Supervising 3D Talking Head Avatars with Analysis-by-Audio-Synthesis', arXiv 2504.13386), making this a notable node in their talking-head line.
author_followups: Supervising 3D Talking Head Avatars with Analysis-by-Audio-Synthesis (arXiv 2504.13386, 2025) — same group's contemporaneous follow-up on speech-driven 3D avatars
notable_citers: 2025 speech-driven 3D facial animation / lip-sync papers adopting the speech-mesh perceptual metrics and loss

title: T2V-CompBench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Compositional Text-to-video Generation
lineage_note: By far the highest-impact paper in this set (132 citations within ~1 year). From Xihui Liu's HKU group with CUHK and Huawei Noah's Ark Lab, it is the first benchmark for compositional text-to-video generation (attribute binding, spatial/motion relations, numeracy) with MLLM/detection/tracking metrics and a public leaderboard. It has become a standard evaluation suite cited across the T2V generation literature, including model releases and surveys.
author_followups: Xihui Liu's HKU group continues a prominent line of compositional generation and evaluation work (e.g., T2I-CompBench lineage that this video benchmark extends)
notable_citers: Widely cited by text-to-video model papers (CogVideoX-, Wan-, Sora-era systems) and T2V evaluation/survey works as a compositional benchmark

title: MixerMDM: Learnable Composition of Human Motion Diffusion Models
lineage_note: From the Barquero/Palmero/Escalera human-motion-diffusion group (Universitat de Barcelona / CVC, with King's College London). It presents the first learnable, adversarially-trained composition of pretrained text-conditioned motion diffusion models. The team has a strong recognized lineage in this area — BeLFusion (ICCV 2023) and FlowMDM (CVPR 2024) — so MixerMDM extends a well-established, notable research program even though its own citation count is still early-stage.
author_followups: FlowMDM: Seamless Human Motion Composition with Blended Positional Encodings (CVPR 2024) — same authors' prior motion-composition work
BeLFusion: Latent Diffusion for Behavior-Driven Human Motion Prediction (ICCV 2023) — earlier high-impact paper by Barquero/Palmero/Escalera
notable_citers: 2025 human-motion-generation and multi-person interaction diffusion papers citing it as a model-composition method

title: Generative Photography: Scene-Consistent Camera Control for Realistic Text-to-Image Synthesis
lineage_note: A CVPR 2025 Highlight led by PhD student Yu Yuan under Stanley Chan, the Elmore Professor at Purdue and a prominent computational-imaging researcher. It introduces Dimensionality Lifting and Differential Camera Intrinsics Learning to give text-to-image diffusion physically-meaningful camera control (focal length, aperture, shutter, color temperature) with scene consistency, beating SD3/FLUX. The Highlight status, Purdue press coverage, and Chan's standing place it as a notable bridge between computational photography and generative models.
author_followups: Stanley Chan's group continues active computational-imaging + generative-model research at Purdue building on camera-physics-aware generation
notable_citers: 2025 camera-controllable image/video generation papers citing it for physically-grounded camera-intrinsic control
